title: Epic G: Data, Lexical, Decode Pipeline — gbf-data
description: Corpus governance + lexical/interaction/decode contracts. CorpusManifest with source provenance + dedup + contamination check. LexicalSpec (model-semantic, in ArtifactCore) split from InteractionBundle (interaction policy, in ArtifactAux). DecodeMode/DecodePolicy/DecodeCapabilitySet/DecodeTransformSet/SessionProfile.

comments.author: bkase
comments.text:
## Epic G: Data, Lexical, Decode Pipeline (`gbf-data`)

### What this is

The corpus governance + lexical/interaction/decode contracts that frame the model's inputs and outputs:
- `gbf-data` corpus pipeline with `CorpusManifest`, source provenance, dedup, contamination check vs `WorkloadManifest`.
- `LexicalSpec` (charset + normalization + control tokens — model-semantic, lives in `ArtifactCore`) and `InteractionBundle` (keyboard layout + transcript spec + session profile — interaction policy, lives in `ArtifactAux`).
- Decode infrastructure: `DecodeMode`, `DecodePolicy`, `DecodeCapabilitySet`, `DecodeTransformSet`, `SessionProfile`.

### Why this matters

Per planv0.md line 703: the old monolithic `InputSpec` is replaced by these split types. `LexicalSpec` is what the model is trained against; `InteractionBundle` is what the runtime renders. Conflating them means "I changed line wrapping, now the model has a new semantic identity hash" — which is silly.

`gbf-data` is also what stops the corpus from contaminating eval workloads silently. Per planv0.md line 2552: data lineage is a reproducibility peer of build lineage.

### Scope (three features)

- **F-G1 — Corpus pipeline**: `CorpusManifest`, source provenance, deterministic normalization, dedup policy, contamination check, deterministic train/val/test splits.
- **F-G2 — Lexical + Interaction**: `LexicalSpec`, `InteractionBundle`, `KeyboardLayoutSpec`, `TranscriptSpec`. Lives in `gbf-artifact` (LexicalSpec in core, InteractionBundle in aux).
- **F-G3 — Decode**: `DecodeMode`, `DecodePolicy`, `DecodeCapabilitySet`, `DecodeTransformSet`, `SessionProfile`. Decode policy is **session-level**, declared in `SessionProfile`; `ArtifactCore` only declares supported modes via `DecodeCapabilitySet`.

### Critical path

```
F-G1 ─┬─→ training (epic bd-1rb) consumes CorpusManifest
      └─→ workload manifests (Epic E) cross-check via contamination

F-G2 ──→ F-A5.5 (keyboard) consumes KeyboardLayoutSpec
F-G2 ──→ F-A5.4 (text) consumes TranscriptSpec

F-G3 ──→ runtime (Epic D) consumes SessionProfile / DecodePolicy
F-G3 ──→ oracles (Epic C) consume DecodeCapabilitySet for canonical decode semantics
```

### Confidence: HIGH

Mostly schema + deterministic pipelines. The hardest part is contamination check — it needs to run efficiently over a multi-GB corpus.

comments.author: bkase
comments.text:
## Charset decision — Tier 2 locked in

Decision date: 2026-04-27. Owner: bkase.

The v1 LexicalSpec charset is fixed at **80 tokens**: A–Z + a–z + 0–9 + 13 punctuation symbols (` .,!?-':;()\"/`) + newline + 3 control tokens (<bos>, <eos>, <unk>). Mixed case preserved.

Tier choice rationale: pure-Stross / *Accelerando* register requires mixed case + parens + quotes + semicolons + slash. Pure caps-only Tier 1 (~48 tokens) would push the voice toward Asimov-era pulp / computer-terminal aesthetic which is off-genre. Tier 2 cost vs Tier 1: ~+1 KiB embedding ROM and ~+7 % per-token inference time — small relative to one extra MoE expert (~30 KiB).

Detailed charset + normalization specs live on the child beads:
- bd-2ym0 (F-G2): charset, LexicalSpec / InteractionBundle schema, keyboard layout sketch.
- bd-tmaw (F-G1): deterministic normalization pipeline, drop policy, splits, CorpusManifest.

This decision is identity-hashed once F-G2 lands. Future churn requires a new artifact lineage.

title: T-B2.16: Stage 0.5 forbid RepairProposal provenance in chunk
description:
## What
Statically and dynamically enforce that `PolicySource::RepairProposal(_)`
never appears in `policy_resolution.v1` provenance during this chunk.
F-B16 will unblock that variant once the oracle question is resolved.

## Why
`compile_knobs` schema is wired NOW so F-B16 can plug in later, but until
F-B16 lands, no proposal exists and accepting one would be a logic bug.
Per planv0 line ~2948, every repair mutation must be typed, monotone in a
declared finite order, bounded by profile policy, and recorded — none of
which is implemented in this chunk.

## Inputs
- T-B2.13 resolver scaffolding.
- T-B2.14 provenance recorder.

## Outputs
- A static check (compile-time fail or a const assert) that no code path
  in `gbf-codegen::stages::policy` can populate `PolicySource::RepairProposal(_)`.
- A runtime semantic-validator check rejecting any
  `policy_resolution.v1` whose provenance chain contains
  `RepairProposal(_)`.
- Test that intentionally tries to construct one and confirms rejection.

## Pitfalls
- DO NOT delete `RepairProposal(_)` from the `PolicySource` enum — F-B16
  needs the variant to exist. Just forbid its USE in this chunk.
- The schema validator must reject reports containing it; the resolver
  must never emit it.
- When F-B16 lands, it removes the static and runtime check; the schema
  itself does not change.

## Acceptance
- gbf-codegen::f_b2_resolve_policy_no_repair_proposal_provenance_in_chunk
- gbf-report::f_b2_policy_resolution_v1_rejects_repair_proposal_provenance

## Refs
- RFC §2.7 (CompileKnobs schema wired but unrepaired), §7.4 deferred.
- planv0 line ~2948 repair rules.
- bd-3ix F-B16 (BLOCKED on oracle).
- Depends on: T-B2.13.
- Blocks: T-B2.19.

title: T7.1: Define TemporalSwitchDigest and related export types
description: In gbf-artifact: TemporalSwitchDigest { layer: LayerId, same_expert_rate_q8_8: u16, transition_mass: Vec<ExpertTransitionDigest> }. ExpertTransitionDigest { from_expert: ExpertId, to_expert: ExpertId, rate_q8_8: u16 }. ClipSaturationDigest { layer: LayerId, boundary: BoundaryId, saturation_rate_q8_8: u16 }. ExpertPayloadDigest { expert: ExpertId, total_bytes: u32, ternary_bytes: u32, scale_bytes: u32 }. Add these to ExportFacts.
acceptance_criteria: cargo check -p gbf-artifact exits 0; cargo test -p gbf-artifact -- export_facts passes; All 4 digest types in ExportFacts; JSON round-trip
close_reason:
Implemented T7.1 export fact digest schema for router switch-awareness.

Summary:
- Added typed export fact digests in `gbf-artifact/src/export_facts.rs`:
  `TemporalSwitchDigest`, `ExpertTransitionDigest`, `ClipSaturationDigest`, and `ExpertPayloadDigest`.
- Added `RateQ8_8` as a validated 0..=1 q8.8 rate type with overflow-safe ratio conversion.
- Added `BoundaryId` for activation/clip boundaries using the existing `ArtifactPath` validation rules.
- Extended `ExportFacts` with default-empty `temporal_switch`, `clip_saturation`, and `expert_payloads` vectors.
- Made invalid states unrepresentable at the schema boundary where practical: transition mass cannot sum above 1.0, payload ternary+scale bytes cannot exceed total bytes, invalid rates reject in constructors and deserialization.
- Added `LayerId` to `ExpertPayloadDigest` after review because current model/export code uses expert indexes local to expert blocks/layers rather than a documented artifact-global expert id allocation.

Support matrix:
| Claim | Status | Gate / owner |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ExportFacts can carry temporal switch, clip saturation, and expert payload digest categories | Supported here | `cargo test -p gbf-artifact -- export_facts` |
| New digest fields preserve old ExportFacts JSON compatibility when absent | Supported here | `export_facts_default_new_digests_to_empty_for_old_payloads` |
| Public JSON field names are pinned for downstream consumers | Supported here | explicit `serde_json::json!` shape assertion in `export_facts_carry_router_observation_digests` |
| q8.8 rates reject impossible raw values and impossible ratios | Supported here | `export_fact_rates_are_valid_q8_8_fractions` |
| Transition mass is a valid bounded distribution fragment | Supported here | constructor + deserialization checks in `export_fact_transitions_ids_and_payload_bytes_are_validated` |
| Real training-time collection/population of these facts | Not claimed here | `bd-3pg` |
| ExpertSlotAffinity / CompilePreferences consumption | Not claimed here | `bd-2pe` |
| Dashboard/report adoption or compiler placement behavior | Not claimed here | downstream F7/F10 beads |

Review agents:
- Mode 1 Intent: agreed this is the right schema-only problem; flagged layer-local expert identity ambiguity and unrelated `history/planv0.md` worktree edits. Fixed payload layer scope and left unrelated plan/context files unstaged.
- Mode 2 Design: flagged unchecked transition-mass aggregates and unsafe ratio conversion. Fixed both with checked construction/deserialization and `u128` ratio math plus `numerator <= denominator`.
- Mode 3 Semantic: independently flagged the ratio boundary/overflow bug. Added regression tests for `1001/1000`, zero denominators, and `u64::MAX/u64::MAX`.
- Mode 4 Evidence: requested public JSON-shape pinning and schema-only closure scope. Added explicit JSON shape assertions and kept producer/export population claims moved to `bd-3pg`.

Feedback-harness reflection:
| Feedback item | Root cause | Guide | Sensor | Owner/gate |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Ratio helper accepted impossible rates after rounding and risked overflow | Conversion validated after quantization and used mixed saturating/unchecked arithmetic | AGENTS now requires rate/probability newtypes to reject impossible ratios before quantization and use overflow-safe conversion helpers | `export_fact_rates_are_valid_q8_8_fractions` covers `1001/1000`, zero denominator, and `u64::MAX/u64::MAX` | `bd-10d` / `cargo test -p gbf-artifact -- export_facts` |
| Transition mass could exceed 1.0 despite per-entry valid rates | Aggregate invariant was not enforced in constructor or serde | AGENTS now requires distribution-like vectors to validate aggregate invariants in constructors and deserialization | `export_fact_transitions_ids_and_payload_bytes_are_validated` covers constructor and JSON rejection | `bd-10d` / `cargo test -p gbf-artifact -- export_facts` |
| Roundtrip tests did not pin public artifact field names | Serde could read its own renamed shape without downstream compatibility proof | AGENTS now requires explicit JSON shape assertions for public artifact schemas | `export_facts_carry_router_observation_digests` checks exact `serde_json::json!` field names | `bd-10d` / `cargo test -p gbf-artifact -- export_facts` |
| Expert payload facts were ambiguous across layers | Schema assumed global `ExpertId`, while current model code uses layer/block-local indexes | AGENTS now requires expert-scoped export facts to state global vs layer-local identity and include `LayerId`/path when local | `ExpertPayloadDigest` includes `LayerId`; JSON shape test pins it | `bd-10d`; downstream producer `bd-3pg` |
| Schema work could be mistaken for producer adoption | Bead adds durable types, but export path still constructs empty digest vectors today | AGENTS now requires export fact schema beads to separate schema support from producer collection and consumer adoption | Closure support matrix moves collection to `bd-3pg` and preferences to `bd-2pe` | `bd-10d` closure; `bd-3pg`; `bd-2pe` |
| API getters used source/target names while JSON uses from/to | Rust `from_*` getter names conflict with clippy wrong-self-convention | No action: serialized field names are pinned by JSON shape tests; source/target accessors are non-schema convenience API | `cargo clippy -p gbf-artifact -- -D warnings`; JSON shape test | `bd-10d` |

Commands run:
- `cargo fmt --all`
- `cargo fmt --check --all`
- `cargo check -p gbf-artifact`
- `cargo test -p gbf-artifact -- export_facts` (6 passed)
- `cargo clippy -p gbf-artifact -- -D warnings`

Non-goals:

comments.author: bkase
comments.text:
## Agent Instructions

**Target crate**: `gbf-artifact`
**Target file(s)**: `gbf-artifact/src/export_facts.rs` (or `gbf-artifact/src/digest.rs`)

**Reference**: `history/planv0.md` — search for "TemporalSwitchDigest", "ExpertTransitionDigest", "ClipSaturationDigest", "ExpertPayloadDigest", "ExportFacts".

### Implementation Notes
Define these types in gbf-artifact:
```rust
pub struct TemporalSwitchDigest {
    pub per_layer_switch_rate: Vec<f32>,     // fraction of consecutive tokens that switch expert
    pub mean_switch_rate: f32,
    pub sample_count: u64,
}

pub struct ExpertTransitionDigest {
    pub per_layer_transition_matrix: Vec<Vec<Vec<f32>>>,  // [layer][from_expert][to_expert]
}

pub struct ClipSaturationDigest {
    pub per_layer_clip_rate: Vec<f32>,       // fraction of activations clipped
    pub per_layer_max_activation: Vec<f32>,
}

pub struct ExpertPayloadDigest {
    pub per_expert_bytes: Vec<u32>,
    pub total_expert_bytes: u64,
}
```
- Add all four to `ExportFacts` struct as `Option<T>` fields.
- Derive: `Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize`
- These are training-time observations exported with the artifact for compiler hints.

### Acceptance Criteria
```bash
cargo check -p gbf-artifact                       # Compiles
cargo test -p gbf-artifact -- export_facts         # Tests pass
cargo clippy -p gbf-artifact -- -D warnings        # No warnings
# Verify: ExportFacts can carry all four digest types
# Verify: JSON serialization round-trip
```

### Constitution Checkpoints
- §I.1: Digests are typed structs, not raw JSON blobs
- §VI.1: ExportFacts is the single source of truth for training observations

title: T-B1.6.9: Ignored-test discipline guard — plain cargo test must not run heavy N=128 paths
description: Meta-test that asserts each named heavy F-B1 test (N=128 streamed output, L4 hard gate suite, full sweep bench) carries #[ignore]. If a contributor removes the attribute, this test fires and the pre-commit hook rejects the commit with a remediation hint. Establishes the heavy-test discipline pattern that M1's quantized-kernel tests will inherit.

Evidence: gbf-report realism_report.v1 schema/self-hash validation; gbf-bench MatmulI8Bringup streaming-ROM runner and structural-count gates; companion artifacts cycle_attribution_n128.json, matmul_n128.map.json, tile_dump_index_n128.json, frame_trace_n128.json, and streaming ROM sidecar; docs/review/f-b1 packet docs/checklist; scripts/review/f-b1/{smoke,regen,verify-packet}.sh; CI wiring and ignored-test discipline meta-check. title: T-A7.3: DeterminismPolicy — CartridgeRtcMode + SaveStateMetadataMode + PowerOnRamPolicy + AudioOutputMode + fingerprint
description: Typed-enum DeterminismPolicy in determinism.rs with FOUR named-variant fields: cartridge_rtc (CartridgeRtcMode::{Fixed,RealTime} — Fixed pinned to FIXED_CARTRIDGE_RTC_UNIX_MS but APPLIED only when gameroy-core exposes a cartridge RTC hook; loading an RTC-bearing cartridge returns EmuError::Determinism{reason} unless the hook is available; for M0/MBC5 it is unobserved — see RFC §3.7.1 finding 7), save_state_metadata (SaveStateMetadataMode::{Fixed,None} — separate dimension from cartridge RTC; load-bearing for byte-equal snapshot bytes via FIXED_SAVE_STATE_UNIX_MS), power_on_ram (PowerOnRamPolicy::{GameroyDefault, FixedFill{wram:u8, hram:u8, cartridge_ram:u8}} — per-region because gameroy defaults differ per region (0xFF for WRAM/HRAM, 0x00 for cartridge RAM); the Seeded{seed} variant from earlier drafts was DROPPED for M0 since the Game Boy has no built-in RNG and gameroy-core has no seedable power-on-RAM API — finding 7), audio_output (AudioOutputMode::Disabled — single-variant for M0 since the headless gameroy-core has no host audio sink; the with_audio_output_enabled() builder verb was REMOVED — finding 7). APU emulation evolves normally; only host output is suppressed. Default::default() chooses the locked-down variant for all four. DeterminismPolicyBuilder exposes the surviving named opt-out verb with_real_time_cartridge_rtc; with_audio_output_enabled() is gone. fingerprint() -> Hash256 over a stable serialization is the load-bearing method consumed by Snapshot::lineage.policy_fingerprint. Single source of truth across gbf-test, gbf-bench, gbf-debug, and gbf-codegen::trace_build (every Trace build).
close_reason: Completed F-A7 DeterminismPolicy: fixed save-state metadata, RTC rejection for unsupported RTC cartridges, power-on RAM policy, audio disabled mode, stable fingerprint, and determinism tests. Gates: full workspace fmt/clippy/test pass.

comments.author: bkase
comments.text: RFC F-A7 source-of-truth reconciliation: DeterminismPolicy carries cartridge_rtc, save_state_metadata, power_on_ram, and audio_output as named value objects; default is locked down; M0 rejects RTC-bearing cartridges because gameroy-core exposes no headless RTC control; power-on FixedFill is accepted only for BootRom mode; fingerprint is stable and feeds SnapshotLineage. Claim-to-gate: determinism policy unit tests, determinism_single_path, snapshot lineage tests, and workspace gates pass.

title: T-A1.1: ISA types and register/operand modeling
description: Define legal LR35902 instruction shapes, registers (Reg8/Reg16), operand modes (Operand8/Operand16), conditions, RST vectors. The type backbone for everything in gbf-asm — encoder, builder, cycle model, layout all consume Instr from here. Make invalid operand combinations unrepresentable (per CONSTITUTION §I.1).
close_reason:
Implemented the canonical LR35902 ISA type backbone in `gbf-asm/src/isa.rs`.

Scope completed:
- Added typed registers, conditions, RST vectors, bit indices, high/direct address operands, operand-mode classification, and instruction-family variants.
- Split instruction operands into narrower types so illegal families such as immediate destinations, `PUSH SP`, `LD [HL], [HL]`, non-`C` high-register addressing, and out-of-range bit/RST values are not representable through public constructors.
- Added `Instr::byte_len()` as the static sizing contract consumed by layout/cycle-model work.
- Added serde validation for constructor-validated newtypes via `#[serde(try_from = ...)]`, plus negative deserialization tests.
- Documented that this is canonical concrete/post-symbol instruction IR. Symbolic labels, relocations, pseudo-ops, branch relaxation, machine effects, and final opcode emission remain with their owning beads.

Reference checked:
- Pan Docs CPU Instruction Set: https://gbdev.io/pandocs/CPU_Instruction_Set.html

Moved / explicit non-goals:
- Symbolic labels, builder DSL, and pseudo-ops: `bd-3p1`.
- Branch relaxation and symbolic-to-concrete branch lowering: `bd-c4s`.
- Final opcode bytes and invalid-opcode rejection: `bd-2o3`.
- Cycle timing beyond byte length: `bd-2k5`.
- MachineEffect / PrivilegeClass typing: `bd-1bw`.
- Section roles, provenance, and symbols: `bd-1e2`.

Subagent review results and fixes:
- Mode 1 found that `Instr` lifecycle and canonical-vs-raw-legal ISA policy were under-specified. Fixed with module docs and explicit non-goals above.
- Mode 2 found serde-derived invalid-state bypass, ambiguous `Operand16::SP` representation, and unclear `Reg16Math` naming. Fixed by serde `try_from`, `Reg16Pair`/`Operand16::Sp`, and `Reg16Data`.
- Mode 3 independently found the serde invalid-state bypass. Fixed and covered with `isa::serde_rejects_invalid_validated_operands`.
- Mode 4 found the same serde gap and noted that filtered tests can pass with zero tests on old code. Fixed the test paths earlier and added AGENTS guidance requiring closure evidence to confirm filtered tests actually ran.

Feedback-harness reflection:

| Feedback item | Root cause | Guide | Sensor | Owner/gate |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Constructor-validated tuple newtypes derived `Deserialize` | Private fields are still populated by generated serde code | Do not derive `Deserialize` for validated newtypes unless serde routes through validation | `cargo test -p gbf-asm -- isa::serde_rejects_invalid_validated_operands` | `AGENTS.md` ASM / ISA Beads; `gbf-asm/src/isa.rs` |
| `Instr` lifecycle unclear | Task mixed concrete ISA, symbolic authoring IR, and later relaxation concerns | State whether AsmIR is pre-layout symbolic, post-relaxation concrete, or adapter | Module docs plus moved/non-goal closure entries | `bd-11e`, `bd-3p1`, `bd-c4s` |
| Canonical high-memory direct-address policy looked like raw ISA legality | Task conflated legal CPU encodings with canonical project encodings | Distinguish legal CPU encodings from canonical project encodings | `DirectAddr` constructor boundary and operand classification test | `AGENTS.md` ASM / ISA Beads; `isa::operand_classification` |
| Filtered cargo tests passed with zero tests before patch | Test path was easy to cite without proving selection | Confirm filtered commands run nonzero tests in closure evidence | Closure command output shows 1 or 3 tests run | `AGENTS.md` ASM / ISA Beads; closure matrix |
| Unrelated dirty `history/planv0.md` present | Existing worktree change unrelated to bead | Do not stage unrelated files; call it out at final status | `git status --short` before staging/commit | Session protocol |

Claim-to-gate matrix:

| Closure claim | Guarding test or command | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `gbf-asm` compiles with concrete ISA types | `cargo check -p gbf-asm` | Passed |
| Operand modes cover the intended LR35902 operand classes and constructor boundaries | `cargo test -p gbf-asm -- isa::operand_classification` | Passed; ran 1 test |
| Every `Instr` variant has a known static byte length | `cargo test -p gbf-asm -- isa::instr_size_in_bytes` | Passed; ran 1 test |
| Serde cannot bypass validated `BitIndex` / `DirectAddr` constructors | `cargo test -p gbf-asm -- isa::serde_rejects_invalid_validated_operands` | Passed; ran 1 test |
| All local ISA tests pass together | `cargo test -p gbf-asm -- isa` | Passed; ran 3 tests |
| Formatting and linting are clean | `cargo fmt --check --all`; `cargo clippy -p gbf-asm -- -D warnings` | Passed |

Commands run:

comments.author: bkase
comments.text:
## T-A1.1: ISA types + register/operand modeling

**Target crate**: `gbf-asm`
**Target file(s)**: `gbf-asm/src/isa.rs`
**Reference**: `planv0.md` §"Assembly eDSL" (line 2046).

### Background

This is the type backbone for everything else in `gbf-asm`. The encoder, builder, cycle model, and layout passes all consume `Instr` values defined here. Getting this wrong cascades.

### Implementation Notes

```rust
pub enum Reg8 { A, B, C, D, E, H, L }
pub enum Reg16 { BC, DE, HL, SP, AF }    // pairs + special
pub enum Cond  { NZ, Z, NC, C }

pub enum Operand8 {
    Reg(Reg8),
    Imm(u8),
    HlInd,                       // (HL)
    Direct(u16),                 // (nn)
    HighDirect(u8),              // ($FF00 + n)
    HighReg(Reg8 /* C */),       // ($FF00 + C)
}

pub enum Operand16 {
    Reg(Reg16),
    Imm(u16),
    Sp,
    SpPlus(i8),
}

pub enum Instr {
    Nop,
    Ld8 { dst: Operand8, src: Operand8 },
    Ld16 { dst: Reg16, src: Operand16 },
    LdAddrReg16 { dst: Reg16Addr, src: Reg8 /* A */ },  // (BC), (DE), (HL+), (HL-)
    Add { src: Operand8 },
    Adc { src: Operand8 },
    Sub { src: Operand8 },
    Sbc { src: Operand8 },
    And { src: Operand8 },
    Or  { src: Operand8 },
    Xor { src: Operand8 },
    Cp  { src: Operand8 },
    Inc8 { dst: Operand8 },
    Dec8 { dst: Operand8 },
    Inc16 { dst: Reg16 },
    Dec16 { dst: Reg16 },
    Add16Hl { src: Reg16 },
    AddSp { off: i8 },
    Bit { bit: u3, src: Operand8 },
    Res { bit: u3, src: Operand8 },
    Set { bit: u3, src: Operand8 },
    Rlca, Rrca, Rla, Rra, Rlc { src: Operand8 }, Rrc { src: Operand8 },
    Rl { src: Operand8 }, Rr { src: Operand8 },
    Sla { src: Operand8 }, Sra { src: Operand8 }, Srl { src: Operand8 },
    Swap { src: Operand8 },
    JpAbs { cond: Option<Cond>, addr: u16 },
    JpHl,
    JrRel { cond: Option<Cond>, off: i8 },
    Call { cond: Option<Cond>, addr: u16 },
    Ret { cond: Option<Cond> },
    Reti,
    Rst { vector: RstVector },           // $00, $08, ..., $38
    Push { src: Reg16 },
    Pop  { dst: Reg16 },
    Halt,
    Stop,
    Di, Ei,
    Ccf, Scf, Cpl, Daa,
}
```

`u3` is a typed three-bit value (use `bilge` crate or local newtype with `Self::new(u8) -> Option<Self>`).

`Operand8::Direct(u16)` is **address-typed** — the encoder rejects reads from `$FF00–$FFFF` through `Direct` because those should use `HighDirect` for the smaller encoding. This is a "make invalid states unrepresentable" call.

### Acceptance Criteria

```bash
cargo check -p gbf-asm                                              # Compiles
cargo test -p gbf-asm -- isa::operand_classification                 # Operand variants are exhaustive over Pan Docs op modes
cargo test -p gbf-asm -- isa::instr_size_in_bytes                    # Each Instr variant has a known byte length
cargo clippy -p gbf-asm -- -D warnings                               # Clean
```

### Constitution Checkpoints

- §I.1: Invalid operand combinations (e.g., `Ld8 { dst: Imm(_), src: ... }`) are unrepresentable.
- §VI.1: ISA shape lives only here; nothing else encodes opcodes.

title: T5.4: Implement ternary zero regularizer
description: Soft L1-style penalty on the pre-threshold expert weights, encouraging sparsity (more zeros in the ternary representation). This is useful for SparseTernaryBitplanes encoding where higher zero density means fewer bytes. For Ternary2, zeros don't save bytes but they do reduce accumulator magnitude, which is still useful. Config-gated.
acceptance_criteria: cargo check -p gbf-train exits 0; cargo test -p gbf-train -- loss::sparsity passes; All-zero weights -> regularizer=0; Near-zero weights penalized
close_reason:
QAT Closure Checklist

Artifact contract: bd-11s implements a pre-export training-loss helper in `gbf-train::loss::sparsity`; it does not define serialized artifact bytes, SparseTernaryBitplanes savings, or hard ternary export agreement. The local contract is `mean(min(abs(weight), threshold))` over full-precision pre-threshold expert weights. Export/materialization claims remain outside this bead.

differentiable Burn path: `burn_ternary_zero_regularizer` supports a global scalar threshold for any non-empty weight tensor, and `burn_ternary_zero_regularizer_for_matrix` supports row-major `[output_rows, input_cols]` expert weights with one global or one per-output-row threshold. Threshold tensors are detached so the regularizer gradient is honest for weights and cannot game the threshold state.

Tests proving it:
- `cargo check -p gbf-train`
- `cargo test -p gbf-train -- loss::sparsity` (7 passed)
- `cargo test -p gbf-train --features burn-adapter -- loss::sparsity` (15 passed)
- `cargo clippy -p gbf-train --features burn-adapter -- -D warnings`
- `python3 scripts/qat_harness_checks.py`
- `cargo test -p gbf-test --test architecture` (3 passed)

Acceptance movement: The acceptance wording “toward 0 or ±scale” is narrowed to the zero-regularizer behavior proved here: sub-threshold non-zero weights receive shrink-to-zero gradients, already-active weights are not pulled downward by this term, and hard ternary projection/export behavior is not claimed. Training-loop/config-composer adoption of `lambda_zero` is moved to `bd-1c6`.

Support Matrix

| Public behavior or variant | Scalar/model core | Burn training path | Export/artifact path | Guard |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Global-threshold capped L1 zero regularizer | supported | supported | not applicable | `cargo test -p gbf-train --features burn-adapter -- loss::sparsity` |
| Per-output-row matrix thresholds | supported | supported | not applicable | `ternary_zero_regularizer_supports_global_and_per_output_row_thresholds`, `burn_ternary_zero_regularizer_supports_per_output_row_thresholds` |
| Per-weight threshold public API | rejected | rejected | not applicable | no public helper; threshold-count rejection tests guard row contract |
| `lambda_zero` training-loop config gate | moved | moved | not applicable | moved to `bd-1c6`; weighted helpers validate raw diagnostics |
| SparseTernaryBitplanes byte savings/export agreement | not applicable | not applicable | moved | artifact/export owners, not bd-11s |

Claim-To-Gate

| Closure claim | Guarding test or command | Feature gate | Notes or deviation |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| All-zero weights produce zero raw loss | `cargo test -p gbf-train -- loss::sparsity` | none | `ternary_zero_regularizer_is_zero_for_all_zero_weights` |
| More non-zero near-zero weights increase the raw zero penalty | `cargo test -p gbf-train -- loss::sparsity` | none | `ternary_zero_regularizer_penalty_increases_with_near_zero_nonzero_weights` |
| Sub-threshold weights shrink toward zero and above-threshold weights have zero regularizer gradient | `cargo test -p gbf-train --features burn-adapter -- loss::sparsity` | `burn-adapter` | scalar finite-difference and Burn autodiff tests prove the local regularizer gradient, not ±scale attraction |
| Burn capped-L1 math preserves scalar parity for large finite weights | `cargo test -p gbf-train --features burn-adapter -- loss::sparsity` | `burn-adapter` | regression covers the cancellation bug found in semantic review |
| Matrix thresholds match the QAT per-output-row threshold contract | `cargo test -p gbf-train --features burn-adapter -- loss::sparsity` | `burn-adapter` | scalar and Burn tests reject unsupported threshold counts |
| Weighted helpers do not hide invalid raw diagnostics at `lambda_zero == 0` | `cargo test -p gbf-train --features burn-adapter -- loss::sparsity` | `burn-adapter` | scalar and Burn tests reject NaN raw loss even when lambda is zero |
| Reflection guiderails were added for repeated loss-review failures | `git diff --check`; `cargo clippy -p gbf-train --features burn-adapter -- -D warnings` | none / `burn-adapter` | `AGENTS.md` now records capped-loss, ternary-threshold, and weighted-helper rules |

No-future

No future threshold variants are accepted by this bead. Public matrix helpers accept only one global threshold or one threshold per output row. Learned-per-group thresholds, per-weight thresholds, SparseTernaryBitplanes byte savings, and training-loop `lambda_zero` adoption are not silently accepted here.

Feedback Harness Reflection

| Feedback item | Root cause | Guide | Sensor | Owner/gate |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Gradient acceptance overread as “toward 0 or ±scale” | The bead wording mixed zero regularization with broader QAT projection dynamics | Closure now states the narrower shrink-to-zero/no-downward-active-weight claim | Scalar finite-difference and Burn gradient tests | `cargo test -p gbf-train --features burn-adapter -- loss::sparsity` |
| Per-weight threshold API broadened the public model contract | Initial helper exposed a threshold shape not owned by `TernaryLinearQat` | `AGENTS.md` says ternary zero matrix thresholds must be global or per-output-row | Scalar/Burn row-threshold tests plus threshold-count rejection tests | `gbf-train/src/loss/sparsity.rs`; `cargo test -p gbf-train --features burn-adapter -- loss::sparsity` |
| Burn capped-L1 formula lost small thresholds for large finite weights | Algebraic `min` used subtractive cancellation | `AGENTS.md` says capped tensor losses use clamp or mask selection, with large finite parity regression | `burn_ternary_zero_regularizer_preserves_large_finite_threshold_cap` and per-row large finite test | `cargo test -p gbf-train --features burn-adapter -- loss::sparsity` |
| `lambda_zero == 0` helper masked invalid raw diagnostics | Weighted helper was acting like a composer/config gate | `AGENTS.md` distinguishes raw weighted-loss helpers from contribution/composer helpers | Scalar and Burn NaN-raw-at-zero-lambda rejection tests | `cargo test -p gbf-train --features burn-adapter -- loss::sparsity`; config adoption owner `bd-1c6` |

comments.author: bkase
comments.text:
## Agent Instructions

**Target crate**: `gbf-train`
**Target file(s)**: `gbf-train/src/loss/sparsity.rs`

**Reference**: `history/planv0.md` — search for "ternary zero regularizer", "L1", "sparsity".

### Implementation Notes
- Soft L1-style penalty on pre-threshold expert weights, encouraging sparsity (more zeros in ternary encoding).
- `pub fn ternary_zero_regularizer(weights: &Tensor, threshold: &Tensor) -> Tensor`
- Penalty: `Σ |w| * (1 - sigmoid(alpha * (|w| - threshold)))` — smooth version that penalizes weights near threshold
- Or simpler: `Σ min(|w|, threshold)` — encourages weights to be exactly 0 or above threshold
- Config-gated: only active when `lambda_zero > 0.0`.

### Acceptance Criteria
```bash
cargo check -p gbf-train                       # Compiles
cargo test -p gbf-train -- loss::sparsity       # Tests pass
# Verify: all-zero weights -> regularizer = 0
# Verify: penalty increases with number of near-zero weights
# Verify: gradient encourages weights toward 0 or ±scale
```

### Constitution Checkpoints
- §I.2: Pure function
- §I.5: Differentiable w.r.t. weights — honest gradient

comments.author: bkase

title: T7.3: Implement temporal smoothness regularization in router
description: In Top1RouterQat, maintain a running buffer of the previous token's soft routing distribution within each training sequence. Compute L_switch = (1/LT) sum_{l,t} (1 - dot(p_{l,t}, p_{l,t-1})) over contiguous windows. Handle sequence boundaries (mask out cross-sequence penalties). Multiply by lambda_switch. Gradient flows through the router's softmax output. Window size is a config parameter (default: 32 tokens).
acceptance_criteria: cargo check -p gbf-model exits 0; cargo test -p gbf-model -- qat::router::temporal passes; Buffer resets at sequence boundaries; Smoothness in [0,1]

comments.author: bkase
comments.text:
## Agent Instructions

**Target crate**: `gbf-model`
**Target file(s)**: `gbf-model/src/qat/router.rs` (extend Top1RouterQat)

**Reference**: `history/planv0.md` — search for "temporal smoothness regularization", "L_switch", "consecutive tokens", "routing distribution buffer".

### Implementation Notes
- In Top1RouterQat, maintain a running buffer of previous token's soft routing distribution.
- Compute `L_switch` (temporal switch loss) over contiguous windows.
- Window size is a config parameter (default: 32 tokens).
- Buffer management:
  - Ring buffer of size `window_size` per layer
  - Reset at sequence boundaries
  - `pub fn compute_temporal_smoothness(&self, current_probs: &Tensor) -> Tensor`
- This is in the MODEL crate (not loss crate) because it requires router-internal state (the distribution buffer).
- The actual loss scalar computation may delegate to `gbf-train/src/loss/switch.rs`.

### Acceptance Criteria
```bash
cargo check -p gbf-model                         # Compiles
cargo test -p gbf-model -- qat::router::temporal  # Tests pass
# Verify: buffer tracks previous routing distributions
# Verify: window respects sequence boundaries
# Verify: smoothness metric is in [0, 1] range
```

### Constitution Checkpoints
- §I.2: Buffer state is isolated in the router; smoothness computation is pure
- §V.2: Buffer is reconstructable — reset at sequence boundaries, no persistent state needed across restarts

title: T13.4: Dense vs MoE comparison report
description: Emit a DenseVsMoeComparisonReport JSON with both runs' bpc, deployed bytes, projected fit, schedule cost, and the parity verdict. Lives in gbf-report. Consumed by F-C4 conformance and the v0 success envelope's downstream-MoE gate.

title: T10.14: ExportVisitor-to-ArtifactOracle agreement test
description:
Tests that the exported artifact, when evaluated by ArtifactOracle, produces the same outputs as the training model's fake-quant forward pass.

**Forward agreement test:**
- Create tiny model, run forward pass on 4 fixture prompts, record logits L_train
- Export to ModelArtifact
- Load artifact into ArtifactOracle (exact canonical evaluation, no tiling/banking)
- Run ArtifactOracle on same 4 prompts, record logits L_artifact
- ASSERT: L_train == L_artifact (exact equality, not approximate — fake-quant forward should match)

**Checkpoint-level agreement:**
- At each SemanticCheckpointId (PostEmbedding, PostRouter, PostExpertDowncast, PostLogits, PostDecode):
  - Record intermediate values from training forward pass
  - Record intermediate values from ArtifactOracle
  - ASSERT: exact match

**Edge case: different decode modes:**
- Argmax decode: verify token output matches between training model and oracle
- TopKTemperature decode: verify logit distributions match (tokens may differ due to RNG, but pre-sample distributions must agree)

**Quantization gap measurement:**
- Evaluate dense teacher on fixture prompts -> logits L_teacher
- Evaluate ternary artifact on same prompts -> logits L_artifact
- Record: max abs difference, mean abs difference, KL divergence
- Log these as structured metrics for conformance.json
- These are NOT assertions (some gap is expected) — they're measurements

This test is the training-side companion to the oracle suite's differential tests.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p gbf-test -- oracle_agreement passes; Forward agreement within atol=1e-5; Checkpoint-level values match; Quantization gap measured
close_reason:
Implemented T10.14 as the training-side export agreement test using the allowed fixture-local fallback evaluator because `gbf-oracle::artifact` is still a stub.

What landed:
- Added `gbf-test/tests/oracle_agreement.rs`.
- The artifact side evaluates only `ArtifactCore` tensors plus `QuantSpec`; it does not call `TinyModel` internals for artifact evaluation.
- Deployable full-precision weights resolve through `QuantSpec::weight_quant`, and ternary projections resolve through `TernaryQuantEntry`.
- Four tiny fixture prompts now compare hard-quant training trace vs exported artifact fallback trace at `PostEmbedding`, `PostRouter` logits/probs/expert ids, `PostExpertDowncast`, `PostLogits`, argmax decode, and TopKTemperature pre-sample distributions.
- Quantization gap measurement records prompt count, logit count, max abs diff, mean abs diff, and per-token/vocab-row KL divergence with an in-memory JSON shape assertion.
- Added focused regressions for passthrough activation semantics and per-token KL aggregation.
- Added oracle/conformance guidance to AGENTS.md.

Support matrix:

| Behavior | Status | Gate / owner |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Exported tiny artifact agrees with training hard-quant path | Supported in fixture-local fallback evaluator | `cargo test -p gbf-test -- oracle_agreement` |
| Checkpoint values match at embedding, router, expert downcast, logits, and helper decode observations | Supported for the tiny fixture trace | `oracle_agreement_training_hard_quant_forward_matches_artifact_evaluator` |
| Argmax decode agreement | Supported as a helper-level derivation from matched logits | `oracle_agreement_training_hard_quant_forward_matches_artifact_evaluator` |
| TopKTemperature agreement | Supported for pre-sample distributions only; RNG token sampling is not claimed | `oracle_agreement_training_hard_quant_forward_matches_artifact_evaluator` |
| Full `gbf-oracle::ArtifactOracle` agreement | Not implemented here because oracle artifact module is a stub | Handoff comment on `bd-c4wg` |
| `conformance.json` emission | Not implemented here; this bead only pins metric shape in memory | Handoff comment on `bd-35l3` |

Fresh-context review results:

| Mode | Feedback | Resolution |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Intent | Task title overstates real `ArtifactOracle`; metrics wording could imply `conformance.json` emission | Closed as fallback evaluator agreement and recorded handoffs to `bd-c4wg` and `bd-35l3` |
| Design | Metrics were structured but not recorded; passthrough activation would clamp if reused | Scoped report emission to `bd-35l3`; fixed passthrough activation and added a regression |
| Semantic | Full-precision weights were too dependent on path naming; KL was prompt-global rather than token-local | Resolved full-precision tensors through `QuantSpec::weight_quant`; changed KL to per-token/vocab rows and added a regression |
| Evidence | Decode checks and metric JSON are narrower than production oracle/report claims | Closure names decode as helper-level evidence and metrics as in-memory shape evidence only |

Feedback Harness Reflection:

| Feedback item | Root cause | Guide | Sensor | Owner/gate |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Fallback evaluator could masquerade as real ArtifactOracle coverage | The real oracle module is unavailable, but the bead title still names it | Project guide now requires fallback evaluators to be named and the real oracle bead recorded | Closure support matrix plus `bd-c4wg` handoff comment | `bd-c4wg`; `br show bd-12c` closure |
| Full-precision artifact weights were resolved by path convention | The first evaluator treated tensor names as the contract instead of `QuantSpec` | AGENTS.md now requires full-precision deployable weights to resolve through `QuantSpec::weight_quant` | `oracle_agreement_training_hard_quant_forward_matches_artifact_evaluator` exercises all full-precision fixture projections through `full_precision_weight_entry` | `cargo test -p gbf-test -- oracle_agreement` |
| Passthrough activation semantics were easy to corrupt in a fallback evaluator | Activation range and eval-mode behavior were coupled too early | Keep activation eval mode semantics explicit in fallback tests | `oracle_agreement_fallback_evaluator_preserves_passthrough_activation_values` | `cargo test -p gbf-test -- oracle_agreement` |
| KL divergence was computed over a whole prompt rather than per token | Prompt logits were treated as one distribution instead of token-major vocab rows | AGENTS.md now requires token-logit metrics to aggregate per token/vocab row | `oracle_agreement_quantization_gap_kl_is_per_token` | `cargo test -p gbf-test -- oracle_agreement` |
| Metric JSON shape could be mistaken for `conformance.json` emission | The test has no report sink | AGENTS.md now says in-memory metric JSON tests do not prove report emission | Handoff comment on `bd-35l3`; no local emission sensor claimed | `bd-35l3` |

Claim-to-gate:

| Claim | Gate |
| --- | --- |
| Focused oracle agreement tests pass | `cargo test -p gbf-test -- oracle_agreement` |
| New fallback evaluator and regressions are warning-free | `cargo clippy -p gbf-test -- -D warnings` |
| `gbf-test` remains green | `cargo test -p gbf-test` |
| Formatting is unchanged | `cargo fmt --check --all` |

comments.author: bkase
comments.text: EXTERNAL DEPENDENCY NOTE: This test assumes ArtifactOracle (from gbf-oracle) exists and can evaluate a frozen artifact in canonical logical form. ArtifactOracle is part of the compiler/oracle infrastructure (M1-M2 milestone), not part of this training-contract epic. If ArtifactOracle is not yet available when this test is reached, implement the test with a lightweight 'training-side artifact evaluator' that replays the fake-quant forward pass using the exported CanonicalTensors and TernaryWeightPlan, then upgrade to the real ArtifactOracle when it's ready. The key invariant being tested — export agreement with training — is the same either way.
