title: T-B2.21: F-B2 review-packet sub-bundle
description:
## What
Author the F-B2 portion of the chunk's review packet under
`docs/review/f-b2-f-b4/`: scope.md, architecture.md, claim-to-gate.md,
reviewer-checklist.md, known-debt.md, reproducibility.md,
generated-artifacts.md, bead-map.md, plus checked-in goldens and
fixtures for `artifact_validation` and `policy_resolution`.

## Why
Per RFC §13, the review packet is the durable evidence trail reviewers
walk. Without it, reviewers cannot diff a regression against a baseline,
and future readers cannot trace decisions back to typed artifacts.

## Inputs
- T-B2.11 (Stage 0 working end-to-end with synthetic fixtures).
- T-B2.19 policy_resolution.v1 schema + emitter.
- T-B2.20 StageCache (so verify-packet.sh can validate cache behavior).
- RFC §13 (file list), §13.1 (markdown contents).

## Outputs
- `docs/review/f-b2-f-b4/scope.md` (chunk delivery, deferrals).
- `architecture.md` (data flow, code paths, fixture locations).
- `claim-to-gate.md` (matrix from RFC §11 verbatim).
- `reviewer-checklist.md` (what to manually inspect).
- `known-debt.md` (RepairProposal blocking, gbf-migrate deferral).
- `reproducibility.md` (canonical JSON, self-hash, domain separator).
- `generated-artifacts.md` (file → meaning table).
- `bead-map.md` (T-B2.X → bead ID lookup).
- `artifacts/artifact_validation.golden.json` + `.sha256`.
- `artifacts/policy_resolution.golden.json` + `.sha256`.
- `artifacts/artifact_validation.failure.golden.json` + `.sha256`.
- `artifacts/policy_resolution.failure.golden.json` + `.sha256`.
- `artifacts/artifact_validation.fixture.toml`.
- `artifacts/policy_resolution.fixture.toml`.

## Pitfalls
- Goldens are the OUTPUT of `regen.sh`; do not hand-edit. Any change must
  go through the regenerator.
- The fixture must be tiny enough that reviewers can read the entire
  report in full.
- `verify-packet.sh` runs the success and failure goldens on every PR
  (CI gate); `regen.sh` is invoked only when intentionally bumping schema.

## Acceptance
- `./scripts/review/f-b2-f-b4/regen.sh` (will be authored in T-B4.13)
  regenerates packet on a clean checkout, ending with no diff.
- `./scripts/review/f-b2-f-b4/verify-packet.sh` exits 0.

## Refs
- RFC §13, §13.1, §14 risks (golden rot mitigation).
- Depends on: T-B2.11, T-B2.19, T-B2.20.
- Blocks: F-B2 closure (bd-2fj).

title: T4.5: Implement per-phase QAT hardness control
description: Each QAT module (TernaryLinearQat, ActFakeQuant, NormApproxQat, Top1RouterQat) must support three hardness levels: Off (bypass, use original weights), Soft (fake-quant with temperature-scaled STE), Hard (full quantization with STE). The phase scheduler calls model.set_hardness() at each phase transition. During Off, the QAT module's forward is identity. During Soft, a temperature parameter (annealed during the phase) controls the STE slope.
acceptance_criteria: cargo check -p gbf-model exits 0; cargo test -p gbf-model -- qat::hardness passes; Off=identity, Hard=quantized; Transitions idempotent
close_reason: Implemented per-phase QAT hardness control with live transition proof; see closure comment.

comments.author: bkase
comments.text:
## Agent Instructions

**Target crate**: `gbf-model` (hardness API), `gbf-train` (phase integration)
**Target file(s)**:
- `gbf-model/src/qat/mod.rs` (add `set_hardness` trait/method)
- `gbf-model/src/qat/ternary.rs`, `activation.rs`, `norm.rs` (implement per module)

**Reference**: `history/planv0.md` — search for "QuantHardness", "per-phase QAT hardness", "Off, Soft, Hard".

### Implementation Notes
- Each QAT module supports three hardness levels:
  - `Off`: passthrough (no quantization effect)
  - `Soft`: smooth approximation (differentiable but not exact)
  - `Hard`: exact quantization with STE
- Phase scheduler calls `model.set_hardness(expert_qat, activation_qat, norm_qat)` at each phase transition.
- Trait: `pub trait QatHardnessControl { fn set_hardness(&mut self, level: QuantHardness); }`
- TernaryLinearQat: Off=identity, Soft=soft threshold, Hard=hard ternary+STE
- ActFakeQuant: Off=passthrough, Soft=smooth clamp, Hard=round+clamp+STE
- NormApproxQat: Off=exact norm, Soft=blend(exact, approx), Hard=approx only

### Acceptance Criteria
```bash
cargo check -p gbf-model                         # Compiles
cargo test -p gbf-model -- qat::hardness          # Tests pass
cargo clippy -p gbf-model -- -D warnings          # No warnings
# Verify: Off mode produces same output as the unwrapped operation
# Verify: Hard mode produces quantized output
# Verify: transitions are idempotent (setting same hardness twice = no-op)
```

### Constitution Checkpoints
- §I.1: QuantHardness enum — three states, no stringly-typed levels
- §V.2: Crash-only — hardness state is reconstructable from phase schedule + current step

comments.author: bkase
comments.text: Owner clarification from bd-tnb: this bead owns QAT hardness semantics for TrainPhaseSpec, including QuantHardness::Soft propagation to LinearStateBlock once the training adapter exists. Scalar LinearStateBlock guards such as linear_state_constructor_rejects_incompatible_norm_tile_width remain bd-tnb tests; bd-2uw owns only the phase-hardness control contract that consumes them.

comments.author: bkase
comments.text: Owner clarification from bd-1jk: this bead owns QAT hardness semantics for TrainPhaseSpec, including QuantHardness::Soft propagation to BoundedKvBlock once the BoundedKv Burn adapter exists. Scalar BoundedKvBlock record-layout and state-canonicality guards remain bd-1jk tests; bd-2uw owns only the phase-hardness control contract that consumes them.

comments.author: bkase
comments.text: Moved-acceptance owner note from bd-ptk: this bead owns executable handling for `QuantHardness::{Off,Soft,Hard}` when phase config is applied to QAT modules. It also participates in applying `RouterTrainMode::{SoftTop1,HardTop1}` together with the phase scheduler once the scheduler calls the model hardness/router boundary.

comments.author: bkase
comments.text:
## QAT Closure Checklist

**Artifact contract:** This bead implements the pre-export executable phase-hardness contract for QAT model/training paths. `QuantHardness::{Off,Soft,Hard}` is now model-owned by `gbf-model::qat` and re-exported by `gbf-train::phase`, so training phase config and executable QAT modules share one enum. Artifact materialization and oracle agreement are not claimed here; export/artifact agreement remains with bd-g90/bd-12c.

**differentiable Burn path:** Implemented for Burn adapter paths that exist today. `TernaryLinearBurnQat` supports live `Off` full-precision, `Soft` sigmoid soft ternary, and `Hard` hard ternary with STE. `ActFakeQuantBurnQat` supports live `Off`, `Soft` clamped-input/hard-output blend, and `Hard` round/clamp fake quant. `NormApproxBurnQat` supports live `Off`, `Soft`, and `Hard` for affine-LUT and tile-RMS plans. `ExpertBlockBurnQat` propagates phase hardness to already-constructed expert/shared branches.

**Tests proving it:** See Support Matrix and Claim-To-Gate below. The critical post-review additions are live transition tests on already-constructed Burn adapters and expert blocks, plus scalar/Burn tests for activation Soft and tile-RMS norm hardness.

**Acceptance movement:** Router dispatch remains on `RouterTrainMode::{SoftTop1,HardTop1}` rather than `QuantHardness`, per the bd-ptk owner note already on this bead. Scheduler-level soft-temperature/progress annealing is moved to bd-cmf, which now explicitly owns exact-step phase progress and annealed `QuantHardness::Soft` updates when the training loop applies `TrainPhaseSpec` rows.

## Support Matrix

| Public behavior or variant | Scalar/model core | Burn training path | Export/artifact path | Guard |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `QuantHardness::{Off,Soft,Hard}` shared between model and phase schema | supported | supported | moved | `cargo test -p gbf-model -- qat::hardness`; `cargo test -p gbf-train -- phase`; export identity remains bd-g90/bd-12c |
| `TernaryLinearQat` hardness | supported | supported | moved | `cargo test -p gbf-model -- qat::hardness`; `cargo test -p gbf-train --features burn-adapter -- ternary` |
| `ActFakeQuant` hardness, including distinct `Soft` | supported | supported | moved | `cargo test -p gbf-model -- qat::hardness`; `cargo test -p gbf-train --features burn-adapter -- activation` |
| `NormApproxQat::AffineClipLut` hardness | supported | supported | moved | `cargo test -p gbf-model -- qat::hardness`; `cargo test -p gbf-train --features burn-adapter -- norm` |
| `NormApproxQat::TileRmsThenAffineClip` hardness | supported | supported | moved | `qat_hardness_controls_tile_rms_norm_forward`; `burn_norm_hardness_controls_tile_rms_forward` |
| Expert block phase application via `set_hardness(expert_qat, activation_qat)` | supported | supported | moved | `qat_hardness_applies_to_expert_block_phase_transitions`; `burn_expert_hardness_can_change_after_adapter_construction` |
| LinearState/BoundedKv scalar phase-hardness propagation | supported | moved | moved | `cargo test -p gbf-model -- sequence`; Burn adapters remain bd-2bm4/bd-3arn |
| Router phase behavior | not applicable | not applicable | moved | Controlled by `RouterTrainMode`, not numeric `QuantHardness`; scheduler boundary remains bd-cmf and integration proof bd-14k |
| Scheduler-level Soft temperature/progress annealing | moved | moved | moved | bd-cmf owns phase progress and any annealed Soft parameter update |

## Claim-To-Gate

| Closure claim | Guarding test or command | Feature gate | Notes or deviation |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Model-owned `QuantHardness` has no train/model vocabulary drift | `cargo test -p gbf-train -- phase` | none | Phase TOML round-trip uses the re-exported model enum |
| Off/Soft/Hard ternary scalar behavior is implemented and idempotent | `cargo test -p gbf-model -- qat::hardness` | none | Checks Off full precision, Soft smooth projection, Hard ternary projection, repeated Off |
| Activation Soft is distinct from Hard and Off | `cargo test -p gbf-model -- qat::hardness`; `cargo test -p gbf-train --features burn-adapter -- activation` | `burn-adapter` for Burn | Guards the review finding where Soft had collapsed to Hard |
| Norm affine-LUT and tile-RMS plans honor hardness | `cargo test -p gbf-model -- qat::hardness`; `cargo test -p gbf-train --features burn-adapter -- norm` | `burn-adapter` for Burn | Guards both public norm plan variants |
| Already-constructed Burn ternary adapters can transition hardness | `cargo test -p gbf-train --features burn-adapter -- ternary` | `burn-adapter` | Includes `burn_ternary_hardness_can_change_after_adapter_construction` |
| Already-constructed Burn expert blocks can transition hardness | `cargo test -p gbf-train --features burn-adapter -- expert` | `burn-adapter` | Includes expert/shared branch propagation after adapter construction |
| Sequence scalar blocks receive phase hardness for owned QAT leaves | `cargo test -p gbf-model -- sequence` | none | Burn sequence adapters remain moved to bd-2bm4/bd-3arn |
| Target crate compiles | `cargo check -p gbf-model` | none | Acceptance command passed |
| Clippy is clean for model and Burn training path | `cargo clippy -p gbf-model -- -D warnings`; `cargo clippy -p gbf-train --features burn-adapter -- -D warnings` | `burn-adapter` for train | Both passed |
| Architecture and QAT closure harnesses still pass | `cargo test -p gbf-test --test architecture`; `python3 scripts/qat_harness_checks.py` | none | Both passed before close; harness will be rerun after close |

## No-future Acceptance

No-future unsupported hardness paths are accepted silently. Existing numeric QAT modules either implement `Off`/`Soft`/`Hard` today or are marked moved with a live owner. Router behavior is intentionally not represented as `QuantHardness`; it remains `RouterTrainMode` because it controls dispatch selection rather than numeric quantization. Schedule-level annealing/progress is not claimed by this bead and is moved to bd-cmf.

## Feedback Reflection

| Feedback item | Root cause | Guide | Sensor | Owner/gate |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Leaf-only hardness did not prove phase transitions | Initial tests set hardness before adapter construction | Updated QAT skill: phase hardness/mode beads must prove live transitions on already-constructed model/Burn state | `burn_ternary_hardness_can_change_after_adapter_construction`; `burn_expert_hardness_can_change_after_adapter_construction` | `.agents/skills/qat-bead-closure/SKILL.md`; `cargo test -p gbf-train --features burn-adapter -- ternary`; `-- expert` |
| Activation `Soft` collapsed to `Hard` | No explicit Soft behavior or test | Support matrix now requires each public mode/path to be explicit | `burn_activation_soft_forward_matches_core_scalar_projection`; scalar hardness test | `cargo test -p gbf-train --features burn-adapter -- activation`; `cargo test -p gbf-model -- qat::hardness` |
| Tile-RMS norm ignored hardness | Tests only covered affine-LUT norm | Public plan variants must be enumerated in support matrix | `qat_hardness_controls_tile_rms_norm_forward`; `burn_norm_hardness_controls_tile_rms_forward` | `cargo test -p gbf-model -- qat::hardness`; `cargo test -p gbf-train --features burn-adapter -- norm` |
| Router hardness wording conflicted with `RouterTrainMode` design | Bead text mixed numeric hardness with dispatch mode | Updated QAT skill: keep router dispatch mode and numeric hardness separate unless API unifies them | Phase module docs and support-matrix row; bd-cmf owner comment | bd-cmf; bd-14k future integration proof |
| Unrelated `history/planv0.md` dirty work appeared in review scope | Dirty worktree was wider than bd-2uw | No guide change; existing workflow already says do not stage unrelated user changes | Commit staging excludes `history/planv0.md`, `oracle-context*` | `git status` before staging/commit |

title: T6.3: Implement tied input/output embeddings
description: For char/byte models (vocab_size <= 256), tie the input embedding and output classifier by default. Implementation: a single EmbeddingTied module that provides both embed(token_id) -> d_model vector and classify(d_model_vector) -> logits via weight transposition. Config option to untie when vocab is large. This halves the embedding parameter count and common-bank cost, which is significant for small models.
acceptance_criteria: cargo check -p gbf-model exits 0; cargo test -p gbf-model -- embedding passes; Tied mode shares weight between embed and classify; Untied mode has separate weights
close_reason: Implemented backend-independent tied/untied embedding semantics with review fixes; see closure comment.

comments.author: bkase
comments.text:
## Agent Instructions

**Target crate**: `gbf-model`
**Target file(s)**: `gbf-model/src/embedding.rs`

**Reference**: `history/planv0.md` — search for "tied input/output embeddings", "EmbeddingTied", "char/byte models", "vocab <= 256".

### Implementation Notes
- For char/byte models (vocab_size <= 256), tie input embedding and output classifier by default.
- Single `EmbeddingTied` module that:
  - Holds one weight matrix of shape `[vocab_size, d_model]`
  - `embed(&self, token_ids: Tensor<Int>) -> Tensor` — forward embedding lookup
  - `classify(&self, hidden: Tensor) -> Tensor` — `hidden @ weight.T` to produce logits
- Config flag: `tie_embeddings: bool` (default true when vocab_size <= 256)
- Untied mode: separate embedding and classifier matrices (for future large-vocab use)

### Acceptance Criteria
```bash
cargo check -p gbf-model                        # Compiles
cargo test -p gbf-model -- embedding             # Tests pass
cargo clippy -p gbf-model -- -D warnings         # No warnings
# Verify: tied mode — embed and classify share the same weight
# Verify: untied mode — separate weights
# Verify: classify output shape is [batch, vocab_size]
```

### Constitution Checkpoints
- §I.1: Tied vs untied is a type-level choice, not a runtime flag that could be mismatched
- §II.3: Default tied for byte-level; minimal code for the common case

comments.author: bkase
comments.text:
Implemented bd-2v4 as backend-independent embedding/classifier semantics in `gbf-model/src/embeddings.rs`.

Scope owned by this bead:
- `EmbeddingConfig` with enum-backed `EmbeddingTieMode::{Tied, Untied}`.
- Default tie mode: `EmbeddingConfig::new` uses tied embeddings when `vocab_size <= 256`, untied otherwise.
- Named constructors `EmbeddingConfig::tied` and `EmbeddingConfig::untied` for explicit policy.
- `EmbeddingTied`, which stores one `[vocab_size, d_model]` weight matrix and uses it for both embedding lookup and classifier logits via `hidden @ weight.T`.
- `EmbeddingUntied`, which stores separate embedding and classifier matrices.
- Explicit `EmbeddingOutput` and `ClassifierOutput` wrappers exposing `[tokens, d_model]` and `[batch, vocab_size]` shapes.
- Constructor/runtime validation for nonzero dimensions, shape overflow, weight length, finite weights, token bounds, hidden shape, and finite hidden values.

Scope explicitly not claimed:
- No Burn module, Tensor object identity, or gradient accumulation proof. Those model-instantiation tests remain in existing bd-2qj.
- No artifact/export representation of tied classifier aliases. Created bd-3bf1 to track the export/artifact sharing contract.
- No common-bank measured budget claim. This bead proves structural parameter count at the scalar model layer only.
- No top-level model config boundary connecting embedding `d_model` to block `d_model`; bd-2qj covers model construction tests.

Subagent review disposition:
- Mode 1 intent review flagged that the patch must not claim Burn Tensor sharing, gradient behavior, export aliasing, or common-bank savings. Agreed; closure scope was narrowed and bd-3bf1 was created for export/artifact sharing.
- Mode 2 design review flagged export/artifact sharing and top-level model shape integration as out of scope, and recommended reducing the bool API. Agreed; `with_tie_embeddings(bool)` was removed, named constructors are primary, model integration is covered by bd-2qj, and artifact sharing is tracked by bd-3bf1.
- Mode 3 semantic review found the scalar behavior correct and noted residual model/export gaps. Agreed; those gaps are explicitly not claimed here.
- Mode 4 evidence review requested positive `from_config` tests and a clear non-performance caveat. Agreed; added default-config construction tests and parameter-count assertions, and closure limits common-bank claims to structural scalar counts.

Support matrix:

| Behavior | Status | Owner/gate |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Byte/char default ties embeddings for `vocab_size <= 256` | Supported | `cargo test -p gbf-model -- embedding` |
| Large vocab defaults to untied | Supported | `cargo test -p gbf-model -- embedding` |
| Explicit tied or untied policy | Supported through named constructors and `EmbeddingTieMode` | `cargo test -p gbf-model -- embedding` |
| Tied embed/classify share one scalar weight allocation | Supported | `embedding_tied_reuses_one_weight_matrix_for_embed_and_classify` |
| Untied embed/classify use separate scalar matrices | Supported | `embedding_untied_uses_separate_embedding_and_classifier_matrices` |
| Classifier logits shape is `[batch, vocab_size]` | Supported | `cargo test -p gbf-model -- embedding` |
| Burn Tensor object identity and gradients | Not claimed here | Existing bd-2qj |
| Export/artifact tied alias representation | Not claimed here | New bd-3bf1 |
| Measured common-bank savings | Not claimed here | bd-3bf1 or later budget/export bead |

Feedback harness reflection:

| Feedback item | Root cause | Guide | Sensor | Owner/gate |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Scalar model module could be overclaimed as Burn Tensor/gradient implementation | Bead wording used Tensor-like language while current `gbf-model` layer is backend-independent scalar semantics | Added `AGENTS.md` guidance requiring scalar model closures to name unsupported Burn/export/artifact/budget boundaries | Closure support matrix separates scalar behavior from bd-2qj Burn/model tests | `AGENTS.md`; bd-2qj |
| Parameter sharing/common-bank saving could be claimed without artifact support | Local pointer aliasing does not imply exported artifact aliasing or bank materialization | Added `AGENTS.md` guidance to pair sharing/count claims with owned-layer sensors and named follow-up owners | Added scalar parameter-count assertions; created bd-3bf1 for export/artifact sharing | `cargo test -p gbf-model -- embedding`; bd-3bf1 |
| Bool API weakened enum-backed mode choice | Initial public constructor accepted a raw bool despite the type-level-choice checkpoint | Added `AGENTS.md` guidance that enum/named constructors should be primary APIs for model modes | Removed `with_tie_embeddings(bool)` and added named constructors/tests | `gbf-model/src/embeddings.rs`; `cargo test -p gbf-model -- embedding` |
| Positive default-to-module construction was untested | Tests checked default enum values and wrong-mode errors but not the happy path from default config into modules | No new guide needed; this is a local coverage gap | Added `embedding_from_default_config_constructs_expected_tie_mode` | `cargo test -p gbf-model -- embedding` |
| Export/artifact aliasing had no bead owner | Existing exporter has separate embedding/classifier paths and no tied alias contract | No closure movement without an owner | Created bd-3bf1 with dependency on bd-2v4 | `br show bd-3bf1` |

Claim-to-gate matrix:

| Claim | Gate |
| --- | --- |
| `gbf-model` compiles with embedding semantics | `cargo check -p gbf-model` |
| Tied mode stores one matrix and uses it for embed and classify | `cargo test -p gbf-model -- embedding` |
| Untied mode stores separate embedding/classifier matrices | `cargo test -p gbf-model -- embedding` |
| Defaults are tied for `<= 256` and untied for `> 256` | `cargo test -p gbf-model -- embedding` |

comments.text:
## REVISION PASS — Cross-cutting testing & logging discipline applied

This bead now blocks on one or more of the new discipline beads added in the audit pass. Specifically:

- **T-B1.0.8** — test fixture infrastructure (synthetic AsmIR, frame traces, expected outputs).
- **T-B1.0.9** — typed tracing event vocabulary + subscriber-level test capture (per AGENTS.md "Logging helper closure must cite subscriber-level capture for event shape").

### Required additions for this bead

1. **Logging discipline**: every event this bead emits or consumes uses T-B1.0.9's typed `FB1TraceEvent` enum, never raw stringly fields. Tests use `CapturedTrace::capture(...)` to assert event shape, not just count.

2. **Test fixtures**: this bead consumes synthetic fixtures from T-B1.0.8 instead of reinventing them. If this bead introduces a new synthetic fixture, it should be added to T-B1.0.8's catalogue with a one-line description in the README.

4. **`run_latest.log.summary.json` cross-reference**: if this bead's behavior is observable in the heavy-gate run (i.e., it emits typed events that the run-log captures), the summary's `tracing_event_counts` field gains an entry for those event types. The diff in that file across runs is the regression signal.

### What did not change

The bead's original specification, types, validation rules, and acceptance gates remain authoritative. This comment adds the discipline overlay; it does not replace anything.

title: F-E2: PlatformCalibrationBundle production
description: Measure bank_switch_cost, sram_page_cost, timer_isr_cost via emulator (F-H3) or hardware. CycleDistribution (mean/p50/p90/p99) + CalibrationConfidence. Layered separately because platform facts are stable across kernel/runtime changes.

comments.author: bkase
comments.text:
## F-E2: PlatformCalibrationBundle production

### What this is

Measure `bank_switch_cost`, `sram_page_cost`, `timer_isr_cost` on a target via emulator (Epic H F-H3 adapters) or hardware. Run instrumented micro-benchmarks; record `CycleDistribution` (mean / p50 / p90 / p99) plus `CalibrationConfidence` (sample_count + class).

### Why this matters

These are *platform-level* facts that don't change with kernel implementations or compiler internals. Layered separately so they don't get invalidated when only kernel impls change.

### Design sketch

```rust
pub fn measure_platform(target: &TargetProfile, adapter: &dyn EmulatorAdapter)
    -> PlatformCalibrationBundle;
```

Bench runners write the bundle to `gbf-store` for compile-time consumption.

### Cross-feature deps

- F-A2.7 calibration schema
- F-H3 emulator adapters

### Confidence: HIGH

title: F-B7: RangePlan (Stage 5)
description: Purely logical: per hot reduction picks SingleI16 / ChunkedI16 / RenormLoop. Proves i16 accumulator safety. Emits certs/range.cert.json.

comments.author: bkase
comments.text:
## F-B5: RangePlan (Stage 5)

### What this is

Purely logical (`planv0.md` line 1242). Chooses a `ReductionPlan` per hot reduction without needing materialization or residency decisions: `SingleI16 | ChunkedI16 { chunk_len } | RenormLoop { tile_len }`. Proves "i16 accumulators are safe here" precisely instead of guessing.

### Why this matters

Reduction structure feeds `StoragePlan` (different scratch needs per plan) and `GbSchedIR` (tile shapes change). Doing it logically before storage decisions keeps the dependency graph clean.

### Design sketch

```rust
pub enum ReductionPlan {
    SingleI16,
    ChunkedI16 { chunk_len: u16 },
    RenormLoop { tile_len: u16 },
}

pub fn plan_ranges(g: &GbInferIR, ranges: &RangeFacts) -> Vec<(NodeId, ReductionPlan)>;
```

Emits `certs/range.cert.json`.

### Cross-feature deps

- F-B3 GbInferIR
- F-B4 ObservationPlan

### Confidence: HIGH

comments.author: bkase
comments.text:
## F-B1: Compute Bringup (M0.5)

### Background

F-B1 is the **M0.5 compute bringup** milestone between the M0 foundation stack (F-A1..F-A8) and M1's first model-facing pipeline. It exists to retire **operational risk** before M1 simultaneously brings up the artifact stratum, the denotational stratum, the first quantized dense kernel, the first `conformance.json`, and the real `CompileRequest`.

The reasoning is structural: without F-B1, M1 would commit to all five strata against an unproven runtime path. Every M1 failure would have too many candidate causes. F-B1 collapses the candidate space by proving the runtime/banking/ROM/emulator/harness substrate can host sustained compute *before* M1 layers semantics on top.

### What F-B1 actually does

F-B1 brings up one workload: tiled `i8 × i8 → i32` dense square matrix multiply, with A and B in distinct switchable ROM banks, executed by the real cooperative Bank0 runtime, yielding often enough that a VBlank-driven UI widget updates every frame, verified byte-for-byte against an independent host-Rust reference in `gbf-verify`.

- Headline run: `N = 128`. Each operand is exactly 16 KiB → fills a full 16 KiB ROM bank.
- Sweep: `N ∈ {32, 64, 96, 128}` for the final report.
- No quantization. No oracle. No `conformance.json`. No `ArtifactCore`. No multi-op IR.

### Why dense matmul, not a baby NN

A baby NN would prematurely pull in artifact semantics, denotational semantics, quantization policy, checkpointed observations, decode policy, and conformance envelopes. Matmul lets us exercise every infrastructure seam — codegen, banking, scheduler, harness, emu, report — without dragging in artifact-stratum semantics. Picking matmul keeps the milestone cleanly *operational*.

### What F-B1 retires for M1

By the time M1 begins:

- A request-shaped bringup path (`ComputeBringupRequest → GbInferIR → ...skeletal plans... → AsmIR → ROM → emu → harness`) is **known to work**.
- The cooperative scheduler is **known to host** sustained compute at 60 Hz cooperative cadence.
- F-A4's BankLease ABI is **known to survive** a real cross-bank tile loop.
- The `realism_report.v1.json` numbers tell M1 exactly how many cycles a product-accumulate costs, how much ROM bandwidth is available, and how many tiles fit in a frame budget. M1's `QuantSpec` choice is data-driven.
- The host-Rust reference pattern (and its placement in `gbf-verify`) is **known to work** as a witness mechanism. M1 promotes it to oracle witness.

F-B1 retires operational risk, not the M1 commitments themselves. M1 still owns the real quantized dense kernel and the real `CompileRequest`.

### Closure conditions (all must hold)

1. ROM executes the matmul through the real runtime/harness/emulator path.
2. A and B read through distinct ROM banks using BankLease/BankGuard only — no raw MBC writes in generated code.
3. Output streamed tile-by-tile through the existing F-A3 harness dump path (no new `HarnessOp` variant).
4. Full output exactly matches `gbf-verify::matmul_reference_i8`.
5. VBlank progress widget updates once per gated frame with `frame_service_misses == 0`.
6. `yield_while_bank_lease_active_count == 0` over the whole run.
7. Deterministic `realism_report.v1.json` baseline checked in under `docs/review/f-b1/artifacts/`, reproducible via `./scripts/review/f-b1/regen.sh` with diff-clean re-run.

If the `N = 128` frame gate cannot be met after iterating in-scope knobs, F-B1 does **not** silently close. The owner either keeps the bead open and iterates, or lands an explicit RFC amendment that changes the closure target and records the measured ceiling as the deliverable.

### Layer plan (L0 → L5)

| Layer | Task    | Size        | What it isolates                              |
|-------|---------|-------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| L0    | T-B1.1  | N = 16      | M0 infra: encoder, emu, runtime, harness      |
| L1    | T-B1.2  | N = 16      | New IR + codegen path at tiny size            |
| L2    | T-B1.3  | N = 64      | F-A4 BankLease ABI under real cross-bank load |
| L3    | T-B1.4  | N = 128     | Tile schedule, output spill, bank-switch budget |
| L4    | T-B1.5  | N = 128     | Cooperative scheduler under sustained compute |
| L5    | T-B1.6  | sweep       | Instrumentation, report, packet               |

L0–L5 are layered for failure isolation. Each gate proves something specific so a regression points at one stage. T-B1.0 is a parallel "foundations" task family that all layers consume (reference, fixtures, table generators).

### Hard architectural decisions (load-bearing)

These are the decisions reviewers must defend, listed because future-self will second-guess them:

1. **Workload is dense matmul, not a baby NN.** Anything model-shaped pulls in artifact-stratum semantics M1 owns.
2. **`i8 × i8 → i32` exact, no saturation.** Worst case `128³ = 2,097,152` fits `i32`. Saturation hides errors; conformance is byte-exact.
3. **Operands in distinct nonzero ROM banks.** Forces real BankLease pressure. Same-bank operands have no banking story.
4. **Multiply table in Bank 0, never switched.** Sequential hot-path read; switching it would dwarf compute.
5. **Kernel in Bank 0, not switchable ROM.** Code switching its own ROM window is unsound. Bank 0 keeps banking simple.
6. **No new `HarnessOp` variant.** Reuse F-A3's existing arena/dump operation. Surface change inflation is refused.
7. **Hard 60 Hz cooperative-frame-service gate.** Soft-gate variant is refused; surfacing the cost ceiling *is* the milestone deliverable if hit.
8. **Reference lives in `gbf-verify`. Shape types live in `gbf-abi::compute_shape` (or `gbf-ir::shape`).** Production crates must NOT depend on `gbf-verify`.
9. **Yield quantum is per-k-lane (KLaneFullTile = 256 products), with `KLaneRows4` (64 products) as fallback.** Yielding only at K-tile boundary (4096 products) is too coarse for a Game Boy.
10. **Output streamed tile-by-tile through harness.** Full 65,536-byte output does not fit standard SRAM; on-cart requantization would prejudice the realism numbers.

### Anti-patterns to refuse on sight

- Adding `quant_policy` field to the request (even Optional).
- Stubbing a `DenotationalOracle::for_matmul()`.
- Emitting an empty `conformance.json`.
- Generalizing `MatmulI8` to `Matmul<T>`.
- Adding `RangePlan` slots.
- Tolerance-based output comparison.
- Putting kernel in switchable ROM.
- Adding any new `HarnessOp` variant.
- Direct MBC writes in generated code.
- Yielding while a `BankLease` is active.
- Storing full output in SRAM.
- Moving multiply table to a switchable bank.

### Dependencies on other features

- **F-A1** (gbf-asm, merged) — encoder for the kernel.
- **F-A2** (gbf-hw, merged) — DMG/MBC5 target profile.
- **F-A3** (gbf-abi, merged) — `HarnessOp` arena/dump op, `LivenessCounters`, `BuildIdentityBlock`.
- **F-A4** (BankLease ABI, in flight) — required for L2 (T-B1.3) and beyond.
- **F-A5** (Bank0 cooperative runtime) — required for L4 (T-B1.5) and L5; if not closed, F-B1 owns the minimal subset needed.
- **F-A7** (gbf-emu, in flight) — required for all emulator integration tests.

### Children (T-B1.0 ... T-B1.6)

- **T-B1.0** Foundations — shape types, reference matmul, fixture generators, quarter-square production + reference table generators, parity tests. Cross-cutting prerequisite for L0 onward.
- **T-B1.1** L0 in-WRAM smoke (N = 16, no banking).
- **T-B1.2** L1 request-to-AsmIR pipeline (uses `WramSmoke` operand layout at N = 16).
- **T-B1.3** L2 cross-bank operands via BankLease (N = 64, distinct ROM banks).
- **T-B1.4** L3 full N=128 tiling, output streamed.
- **T-B1.5** L4 cooperative yielding + frame gate.
- **T-B1.6** L5 realism report + review packet + closure skill.

### Reference

- RFC: `history/rfcs/F-B1-compute-bringup.md` (canonical pinned version).
- Plan: `history/planv0.md` M0/M1 boundary.
- Constitution: §I correctness by construction; §III shifting left; §IV.3 reproducible builds; §V observability; §VI single source of truth.

### Confidence

- **HIGH** on overall shape, layered approach, and refusal list.
- **MEDIUM** on whether the 60 Hz hard gate holds at N=128 — that is precisely what F-B1 measures. If it fails, the waiver path is an RFC amendment, not silent closure.
- **HIGH** on the type-ownership decision (shape types in `gbf-abi`/`gbf-ir`, not `gbf-verify`).

## Agent Instructions (Feature)

**This is a feature bead.** Implement via child tasks T-B1.0–T-B1.6 in the listed order. F-B1 ships as one PR; the PR's commit history is layer-ordered, but `main` receives only the complete integrated milestone.

### Feature Definition of Done

```bash
# Foundations
cargo test -p gbf-verify -- matmul_reference_i8_known_fixture
cargo test -p gbf-codegen -- quarter_square_table_matches_verify
cargo test -p gbf-codegen -- quarter_square_mul_exhaustive_i8

# Layers L0-L5 (heavy gates ignored in plain `cargo test`; owned by review scripts)
cargo test -p gbf-emu -- f_b1_l0_wram_matmul_dump_matches_reference
cargo test -p gbf-emu -- f_b1_l1_compiled_matmul_matches_reference
cargo test -p gbf-emu -- f_b1_l2_cross_bank_matmul_matches_reference
cargo test -p gbf-emu -- --ignored f_b1_l3_streamed_output_matches_reference_n128
cargo test -p gbf-emu -- --ignored f_b1_l4_n128_no_frame_service_misses
cargo test -p gbf-runtime -- f_b1_l2_no_yield_while_banklease_active

# Report and packet
cargo test -p gbf-report -- realism_report_v1_accepts_checked_fixture
./scripts/review/f-b1/regen.sh
./scripts/review/f-b1/verify-packet.sh
```

### Constitution Checkpoints

- §I correctness by construction: every claim has a gate.
- §III shifting left: gates fire under `cargo test`/review scripts, not after-the-fact in benchmarks.
- §IV.3 reproducible builds: `realism_report.v1.json` is bit-reproducible from pinned inputs (no host wall-clock fields).
- §V observability: report records cycle attribution, scheduler service, lease balance.
- §VI single source of truth: `SquareDim` lives in exactly one crate; production crates do not depend on `gbf-verify`.

comments.text:
## REVISION PASS — Audit Findings + Testing/Logging Discipline

This comment documents the audit pass against AGENTS.md and the testing-and-logging directive. It does not change the original F-B1 description; it adds the discipline layer the original feature spec implied but did not enforce mechanically.

### New beads added (6)

| Bead       | Purpose                                                                 |
|------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| T-B1.0.7   | Crate dependency-direction lint (no `gbf-verify` in production crates)  |
| T-B1.0.8   | Test fixture infrastructure (synthetic AsmIR, frame traces, expected outputs) |
| T-B1.0.9   | Structured tracing event vocabulary + subscriber-level test capture     |
| T-B1.6.9   | Ignored-test discipline guard (heavy tests stay `#[ignore]`d)           |
| T-B1.6.10  | CI workflow integration (nightly + on-merge heavy gate)                 |
| T-B1.6.11  | Run-log capture in `regen.sh` (per-run `.log` + committed summary JSON) |

### How these address audit findings

1. **Type-ownership decision is now mechanically enforced** (T-B1.0.7): a test in `gbf-meta-checks` walks cargo metadata and asserts no production crate has `gbf-verify` in its non-dev dep closure. Prior structure described the rule but did not enforce it.

2. **Validator regressions cannot be silent** (T-B1.0.8): every validator now has a synthetic-violation fixture committed to the repo. If a validator silently degrades to a no-op, the rejection-test fails immediately.

3. **Tracing events are typed, not stringly** (T-B1.0.9): per AGENTS.md "Logging helper closure must cite subscriber-level capture for event shape." `FB1TraceEvent` enum + `CapturedTrace::capture()` test helper centralize the vocabulary. All event-emitting beads now depend on T-B1.0.9.

4. **Heavy tests cannot accidentally run on every commit** (T-B1.6.9): meta-test asserts each named heavy test is `#[ignore]`d.

5. **Heavy gates do not atrophy** (T-B1.6.10): scheduled CI runs the full review-packet regeneration on a nightly schedule and on-merge for relevant paths.

6. **Heavy-gate failures are forensically reconstructible from the artifact alone** (T-B1.6.11): every `regen.sh` run produces a per-run tracing log (CI-uploaded, 30-day retention) and a small committed summary JSON with `tracing_event_counts` for diff-friendly review.

### Logging discipline applied across F-B1 (cross-cutting)

Every bead that emits or consumes events now carries a "Logging discipline" section in its comment, with these rules:

- Producer call sites use the typed event helper (T-B1.0.9), never raw stringly fields.
- Tests assert event SHAPE (typed enum variant + structured fields), not just event count.
- The realism-report aggregator parses tracing logs through T-B1.0.9's typed vocabulary.
- Per-run `.log` files capture the unfiltered trace; `run_latest.log.summary.json` is the diff-friendly committed artifact.
- `RUST_LOG=info,gbf=debug` is the canonical heavy-gate log level.

### AGENTS.md guidance specifically applied

- **"Logging helper closure must cite subscriber-level capture for event shape"** → T-B1.0.9 owns the subscriber; T-B1.3.4, T-B1.3.5, T-B1.5.4, T-B1.5.5, T-B1.6.3 cite it.
- **"Public artifact JSON shape tests should pin downstream field names with explicit `serde_json::json!` assertions, not only serde round-trips"** → T-B1.6.1 (realism schema), T-B1.6.4 (cycle attribution + ROM map), T-B1.6.11 (run summary).
- **"Distribution-like vectors must validate aggregate invariants in constructors and deserialization, not just per-entry scalar bounds"** → T-B1.6.1 enforces `min ≤ mean ≤ max` and `min ≤ p99 ≤ max` on `cycles_remaining_at_*`.
- **"Rate or probability newtypes must reject impossible ratios before quantization"** → applies to `RomBankId`, `SquareDim`, `TileSize` (already covered, now explicitly noted).

### Updated dependency graph summary

- **F-B1 closure (T-B1.6.8)** now blocks on: T-B1.0.7 (dep lint), T-B1.6.9 (ignored discipline), T-B1.6.10 (CI), T-B1.6.11 (run log) — in addition to all previously listed deps.
- **L2 / L3 / L4 trace-asserting beads** (T-B1.3.4, T-B1.3.5, T-B1.5.1, T-B1.5.2, T-B1.5.4, T-B1.5.5, T-B1.6.3, T-B1.4.3) now block on T-B1.0.9 (tracing vocabulary).
- **Validator beads** (T-B1.3.3, T-B1.3.4) and **frame-trace ordering** (T-B1.5.5) and **aggregation** (T-B1.6.3) now block on T-B1.0.8 (test fixtures).
- **L0 / L1 emu tests** (T-B1.1.2, T-B1.2.5) now block on T-B1.0.8 (pinned expected output blobs).

### What did NOT change

- The L0 → L1 → L2 → L3 → L4 → L5 layered structure is unchanged.
- The hard 60 Hz cooperative-frame-service gate is unchanged.
- The "no silent close, escalate via RFC amendment" rule is unchanged.
- The "production crates must not depend on gbf-verify" rule is unchanged — it is now also mechanically enforced.

### Reviewer note

The 6 new beads add ~250 lines of code each on the high end (mostly the run-log summary emitter and the CI workflow). The lints and meta-tests are <100 LOC each. Total added LOC budget: ~1.5 kLOC including comments. This is dwarfed by the savings from preventing one architectural drift event.

No features or functionality have been removed. The feature set is strictly larger than before.

comments.text:
## SECOND AUDIT PASS — Structural-counts gate + dev-loop smoke script

A second audit on top of the 2026-05-03 20:55 revision pass. Two new sub-beads added under T-B1.6, two existing direct deps tightened.

### New beads (2)

| Bead       | Purpose                                                                                  |
|------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| T-B1.6.12  | Runtime structural-counts hard gate per N (products, output_tiles, k_tiles, panel_copies, output_byte_count match exact integer formulas) |
| T-B1.6.13  | F-B1 dev-loop smoke entrypoint script (`scripts/review/f-b1/smoke.sh`) with `RUST_LOG=info,gbf=debug` default |

### Why these two specifically

#### T-B1.6.12 — structural counts as **hard gates**, not sanity bands

RFC §10.5 framed structural counts as "reviewer sanity bands, not gates." Cycle counts are correctly measurement-defined, but **structural counts are exact integer functions of N and tile shape**. Treating them as soft surfaces invites silent miscounting bugs in either the runtime (a kernel that skips a tile) or the aggregator (a parser that drops a trace event). A hard gate fails immediately with a precise per-N diagnostic, instead of letting the bug surface as an opaque byte-exact mismatch from T-B1.4.6 / T-B1.5.6.

The bead is dynamic; T-B1.4.5 (static tile-schedule validator) is the codegen-time complement. Both layers exist for good reason — they catch structurally different bugs.

#### T-B1.6.13 — fast dev-loop entrypoint with verbose tracing

Before this addition, F-B1 had:
- the project-wide pre-commit hook (runs everything; verbose tracing not on by default);
- `regen.sh` (heavy, owns the N=128 gate);
- `verify-packet.sh` (runs regen.sh twice for reproducibility).

There was no F-B1-only fast-loop entrypoint with structured tracing turned on. New contributors would not know which crates' tests to run; existing developers would fall back to ad-hoc `cargo test -p` invocations. `smoke.sh` provides the layered, named, verbose-by-default entrypoint and codifies the L0..L5 boundary in shell. T-B1.6.9 (ignored discipline) protects the boundary so heavy `#[ignore]`d tests cannot drift into smoke.sh.

### Tightened direct deps (2)

- **T-B1.6.2 (bench runner)** now directly deps on **T-B1.0.9 (tracing vocabulary)**. The bench runner emits structured trace events that T-B1.6.11 (run-log capture) consumes; the dep was previously transitive only.
- **T-B1.4.4 (host-side reassembly)** now directly deps on **T-B1.0.9**. Per-tile arrival events should be on the typed FB1TraceEvent vocabulary so they appear in run logs alongside the runtime-side tile lifecycle events.

### Impact on the F-B1 closure path

T-B1.6.8 (frozen baseline) now blocks on the two new beads. The audit chain is therefore:

```text
T-B1.6.8 (frozen baseline)
  ├── T-B1.6.12 (structural counts hard gate, new)
  ├── T-B1.6.13 (smoke.sh, new)
  ├── T-B1.6.11 (run-log capture)
  ├── T-B1.6.10 (CI integration)
  ├── T-B1.6.9  (ignored discipline)
  ├── T-B1.0.7 (dep direction lint)
  ├── T-B1.6.7 (closure skill)
  ├── T-B1.6.6 (review packet markdown)
  ├── T-B1.6.5 (review packet scripts)
  ├── T-B1.6.4 (companion artifacts)
  ├── T-B1.6.3 (aggregation)
  ├── T-B1.6.2 (bench runner)
  └── T-B1.6.1 (schema)
```

### What did NOT change

- L0 → L1 → L2 → L3 → L4 → L5 structure is unchanged.
- Hard 60 Hz cooperative-frame-service gate is unchanged.
- "No silent close, escalate via RFC amendment" rule is unchanged.
- All existing acceptance gates still hold; structural-counts gates are additive.
- Type-ownership decision (`SquareDim` in `gbf-abi`) is unchanged.
- Independence rule between production and reference table generators is unchanged.

### Layer-by-layer logging discipline summary (informational, no scope change)

This summarizes the existing wiring after the revision pass; nothing new in this list — it just makes the audit complete:

| Layer | Trace event class                     | Producer beads                       | Subscriber/consumer |
|-------|---------------------------------------|--------------------------------------|---------------------|
| L0    | minimal — kernel + harness dump only  | T-B1.1.1 (hand-authored kernel)      | T-B1.1.2 emu test   |
| L1    | codegen-time events (lower→ir, lower→asmir, rom emitted) | T-B1.2.4 codegen lowering | T-B1.2.5 emu test, T-B1.6.11 run log |
| L2    | banking events (lease acquire/release, mbc write, shadow update) | T-B1.3.2, T-B1.3.4, T-B1.3.5 | T-B1.3.6 emu test, T-B1.6.3 aggregator |
| L3    | tile lifecycle events (tile entered, complete safe point, dump req/ack) | T-B1.4.3 pause/resume, T-B1.4.4 host reassembly (now direct dep on T-B1.0.9) | T-B1.4.6 emu test, T-B1.6.3 aggregator |
| L4    | frame events (vblank, widget tick, scheduler service, yield return) | T-B1.5.1, T-B1.5.2, T-B1.5.4 | T-B1.5.5 ordering test, T-B1.5.6 hard gate, T-B1.6.3 aggregator |
| L5    | aggregator + structural-counts validator events | T-B1.6.2 (now direct dep on T-B1.0.9), T-B1.6.3, T-B1.6.12 (new) | T-B1.6.11 run-log capture, realism report, smoke.sh |

Total F-B1 sub-bead count after this audit: **47** (was 45).

### Confidence

- **HIGH** that structural counts are correctly modelled as exact integer formulas, not measurement-defined.
- **HIGH** that the three-tier dev script model (pre-commit + smoke.sh + regen.sh + verify-packet.sh) is the right boundary.
- **HIGH** that no feature was lost or simplified — the change set is strictly additive (2 new gates, 2 tightened deps).

comments.text:
## RFC PATCH ROUND — 8-issue audit applied (2026-05-03)

`history/rfcs/F-B1-compute-bringup.md` was patched against an external 8-issue audit. The patches are now in tree; affected beads carry their own revision comments.

### Issues addressed

1. **Operand fixture ownership** — `OperandFixtureSpec::DeterministicAffineV1` introduced; production materializer in `gbf-codegen`, verifier materializer in `gbf-verify`, parity-test gate. Fixes the previously broken claim "production crates must not depend on `gbf-verify`" while production crates needed operand bytes.
2. **Validation scope contradiction** — §6 now explicitly enumerates the bringup validator subset that F-B1 *does* enforce (no full M1 validation; F-B1 enforces a bringup subset).
3. **Operand offset missing from request** — `BankedOperandPlacement { bank, offset }` and `RomBankOffset(u16)` newtype added; offset+length validated in checked `u32` arithmetic.
4. **Frame-gate instrumentation tightened** — `KLaneRow` (16 products/quantum) added as final fallback; `max_unyielded_compute_mcycles` and `max_bank_lease_hold_mcycles` added as hard gates; `harness_pause_while_bank_lease_active_count` joined the no-yield-under-lease invariant; `gated_frame_start`/`gated_frame_end_exclusive` make the gated-frame interval unambiguous.
5. **Report shape for sweep** — `build_identity` is now per-run inside `runs[i]`; `run_order` array; `toolchain_identity` block; `compute_bringup_request_hash` differs per N.
6. **Report determinism** — Canonical JSON rule (key ordering, no whitespace, deterministic float formatter); `sample_count` now mandatory in distribution stats; rejection rules for missing/duplicate/out-of-order runs and structural-count mismatches; semantic validator recomputes self-hash and rejects mismatches.
7. **Harness tile metadata** — Tile identity is **derived** from canonical schedule + trap count; static layout metadata moves to `matmul_n128.map.json`; optional debug `TileReadyRecord` must use existing F-A3 dump op, not a new `HarnessOp` discriminant.
8. **Waiver path** — Waiver is now an *amended closure path* via RFC amendment, not "ordinary closure with a waiver section." `KLaneRow` joins the iteration ladder; `docs/review/f-b1/l4-iterations.md` records iteration history.

Plus a **rename**: "progress widget" → "frame-service widget" everywhere except `LivenessCounters::progress_epoch` (which is the compute-side counter and stays named).

### Beads with revision comments

| Bead     | Subject                                                    |
|----------|------------------------------------------------------------|
| bd-2p6a  | T-B1.0.3 — fixture split (production/verifier materializers) |
| bd-3i4v  | T-B1.2.1 — request gains `operand_fixture` + `KLaneRow`    |
| bd-g2cr  | T-B1.2.2 — `BankedOperandPlacement` with offset            |
| bd-2z4q  | T-B1.3.4 — invariant extends to harness pause              |
| bd-h5aj  | T-B1.4.3 — tile identity derived; no in-trap metadata block |
| bd-2nbi  | T-B1.4.4 — host derives indices from canonical schedule    |
| bd-3o3w  | T-B1.5.1 — `KLaneRow` lowering path                        |
| bd-3t6x  | T-B1.5.3 — frame-service widget rename                     |
| bd-3eq0  | T-B1.5.4 — `FrameEventEnvelope.seq`, signed remaining mcycles, `ComputeProgressEpochAdvanced` |
| bd-1e6n  | T-B1.5.5 — lexicographic ordering, signed missed-frame margins |
| bd-k14f  | T-B1.5.6 — three new hard-gate items                       |
| bd-3jmz  | T-B1.6.1 — schema rework (per-run identity, structural_counts, sample_count, canonical JSON, semantic validator) |
| bd-37tp  | T-B1.6.6 — review packet adds `l4-iterations.md`           |
| bd-3qup  | T-B1.6.12 — overlap with new semantic validator (kept distinct: T-B1.6.12 fails fast at bench time before report emission) |

### What did NOT change

- L0..L5 layered structure.
- 60 Hz cooperative-frame-service hard gate.
- Three-tier dev script model (pre-commit + smoke.sh + regen.sh + verify-packet.sh).
- Type-ownership decision (`SquareDim` in `gbf-abi`).
- Independence rule between production and reference table generators.
- Anti-goal refusal list (it grew by two rows: no-fixture-via-gbf-verify, no-new-harness-opcode-for-tile-metadata).

### Confidence

- **HIGH** that the patch round closes the eight specific issues.
- **HIGH** that no scope was lost; everything is strictly additive (`KLaneRow` quantum, `OperandFixtureSpec`, per-run `build_identity`, three new hard-gate items, two new validators).
- **MEDIUM** that the `KLaneRow` quantum is sufficient; if `KLaneRow` plus scheduler tuning cannot meet the gate, that exit is the M1 architectural finding the milestone exists to surface.

title: T-B2.2: ReportEnvelope + canonical JSON + self-hash in gbf-report
description:
## What
Implement `ReportEnvelope<R>` and the `ReportBody` trait in `gbf-report`,
plus the `canonical_json`, `compute_self_hash`, and `round_trip_self_hash`
helpers. See RFC §7.2.

## Why
Three reports (`artifact_validation.v1`, `policy_resolution.v1`,
`static_budget.v1`) share one envelope and one canonicalizer. Without this
fixed point, each report would invent its own JSON shape and self-hash rule
— exactly the drift this chunk exists to prevent.

## Inputs
- `gbf-foundation::{Hash256, SemVer}`.
- The diagnostic taxonomy from T-B2.1 (for `validate_semantics`).

## Outputs
- `pub struct ReportEnvelope<R>` with `schema`, `schema_version`, `outcome`,
  `report_self_hash`, `body: R`. Wire JSON is flat
  `{schema, schema_version, outcome, report_self_hash, ...body_fields}`.
- `pub trait ReportBody` with `SCHEMA_ID`/`SCHEMA_VERSION` consts and
  `validate_semantics(outcome) -> Result<(), Vec<ValidationDiagnostic>>`.
- `pub fn canonicalize<R>(env) -> Result<Vec<u8>, CanonicalJsonError>`.
- `pub fn compute_self_hash<R>(env) -> Result<Hash256, ReportSelfHashError>`.
- `pub fn round_trip_self_hash<R>(env) -> Result<(), ReportSelfHashError>`.

## Pitfalls
- DO NOT use serde `flatten` for envelope merging. `flatten` and
  `deny_unknown_fields` interact poorly. Use a custom serializer/deserializer.
- Floating-point JSON numbers are forbidden in v1 reports — emit and reject
  them at the canonicalizer level. Use \`_q8_8\` / \`_q16_16\` integer fields.
- All `Hash256` values serialize as \`sha256:<64 lowercase hex>\` strings,
  not byte arrays. The \`sha256:\` prefix is part of the JSON schema.
- Domain separation: object hashes use
  \`gbf:<crate>:<type>:<schema-id>:<schema-version>\\0<canonical-json>\`.
- Self-hash convention: zero the field to all-zeros before hashing; recompute
  on parse and reject mismatches.

## Acceptance
- gbf-report::canonical_json_emitter_is_byte_stable
- gbf-report::canonical_json_rejects_float_values_in_f_b2_f_b4_reports
- gbf-report::self_hash_round_trip
- gbf-report deny-unknown-fields enforced for every report body in tests.

## Refs
- RFC §7.2, §2.4 (self-hash), §2.5 (canonical JSON).
- Wave 1 in RFC §8.5.
- Blocks: T-B2.3, T-B2.19, T-B4.11.

title: T-B1.6.13: F-B1 dev-loop smoke entrypoint script (scripts/review/f-b1/smoke.sh)
description:
## T-B1.6.13: F-B1 dev-loop smoke entrypoint script (`scripts/review/f-b1/smoke.sh`)

**Target paths**:
- `scripts/review/f-b1/smoke.sh`
- `scripts/review/f-b1/README.md` (extended with a clear three-tier table)

### Background

F-B1 has three distinct test entrypoints once landed:

| Entrypoint | Cost | Purpose |
|------------|------|---------|
| `cargo test --workspace --all-features` (pre-commit hook) | seconds | Project-wide gate, runs **all** non-ignored tests across all crates |
| `scripts/review/f-b1/smoke.sh` (THIS BEAD) | tens of seconds | F-B1-only fast tests with verbose tracing — the dev-loop entrypoint |
| `scripts/review/f-b1/regen.sh` (T-B1.6.5) | minutes | Heavy `#[ignore]`d gates: full N=128 sweep, L4 hard gate suite |
| `scripts/review/f-b1/verify-packet.sh` (T-B1.6.5) | 2× regen.sh | Reproducibility check (runs regen.sh twice, asserts diff-clean) |

Without `smoke.sh`, a developer making an F-B1 change has two choices: (a) run the entire workspace test suite, paying time on unrelated tests, or (b) remember the exact `cargo test -p <crate> -- <pattern>` invocations for each F-B1 test. Both create friction. New contributors hit the friction first.

### What `smoke.sh` does

Runs all F-B1-related non-ignored tests in a single command, with verbose structured tracing turned on, and prints a compact summary at the end.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# F-B1 dev-loop smoke entrypoint.
#
# Runs every non-ignored F-B1 test across the involved crates with
# verbose tracing. Distinct from regen.sh (heavy N=128 path). Use this
# while iterating on F-B1; use regen.sh before opening a PR.
set -euo pipefail

cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"

# Verbose structured tracing for human review.
export RUST_LOG="${RUST_LOG:-info,gbf=debug}"
export RUST_BACKTRACE="${RUST_BACKTRACE:-1}"

# Time the run so devs can spot drift if smoke.sh starts taking minutes.
START=$(date +%s)

run_block() {
  local label="$1"; shift
  echo
  echo "::: $label"
  "$@"
}

# Foundations (T-B1.0.*).
run_block "F-B1.0 SquareDim shape types" \
  cargo test -p gbf-abi -- compute_shape::

run_block "F-B1.0 Reference matmul + fixtures" \
  cargo test -p gbf-verify -- matmul::

run_block "F-B1.0 Quarter-square production + reference + parity + exhaustive" \
  cargo test -p gbf-codegen -- quarter_square_

run_block "F-B1.0 Crate dep-direction lint" \
  cargo test -p gbf-meta-checks -- production_crates_do_not_depend_on_gbf_verify

run_block "F-B1.0 Test fixture infrastructure" \
  cargo test -p gbf-codegen -- test_fixtures::

run_block "F-B1.0 Tracing event vocabulary + subscriber capture" \
  cargo test -p gbf-runtime -- trace::

# Layers L0..L2 + L3 static checks (heavy L3/L4 emu tests stay ignored).
run_block "F-B1.1 L0 in-WRAM smoke" \
  cargo test -p gbf-emu -- f_b1_l0_

run_block "F-B1.2 L1 request-to-AsmIR pipeline" \
  cargo test -p gbf-codegen -- f_b1_l1_ \
                              -- compute_bringup_request_

run_block "F-B1.2 L1 IR + emu" \
  cargo test -p gbf-ir  -- f_b1_matmul_ir_

run_block "F-B1.2 L1 emu test" \
  cargo test -p gbf-emu -- f_b1_l1_

run_block "F-B1.3 L2 cross-bank static checks + emu (N=64)" \
  cargo test -p gbf-codegen -- f_b1_l2_ \
                              -- f_b1_rom_window_plan_

run_block "F-B1.3 L2 banking runtime checks" \
  cargo test -p gbf-runtime -- f_b1_l2_ \
                              -- f_b1_max_active_bank_leases_ \
                              -- f_b1_mbc_register_write_count_

run_block "F-B1.3 L2 emu (N=64)" \
  cargo test -p gbf-emu -- f_b1_l2_

# L3 static checks (heavy emu test is ignored, owned by regen.sh).
run_block "F-B1.4 L3 static tile schedule + arena" \
  cargo test -p gbf-codegen -- f_b1_l3_

run_block "F-B1.4 L3 host reassembly fast tests" \
  cargo test -p gbf-bench -- f_b1_tile_reassembly_

# L4 fast tests (widget render, frame-trace synthetic ordering).
run_block "F-B1.5 L4 widget + liveness fast tests" \
  cargo test -p gbf-runtime -- f_b1_progress_widget_ \
                              -- liveness_

run_block "F-B1.5 L4 frame-event vocabulary + ordering on synthetic traces" \
  cargo test -p gbf-emu -- f_b1_frame_event_trace_orders_vblank_and_widget

# L5 schema + aggregator + ignored discipline.
run_block "F-B1.6 L5 schema + aggregation fast tests" \
  cargo test -p gbf-report -- realism_report_v1_ \
                              -- structural_counts_

run_block "F-B1.6 L5 bench runner formula tests" \
  cargo test -p gbf-bench -- f_b1_structural_counts_formula_

run_block "F-B1.6 L5 ignored discipline meta-test" \
  cargo test -p gbf-meta-checks -- ignored_discipline_

END=$(date +%s)
echo
echo "F-B1 smoke OK in $((END - START))s. RUST_LOG=$RUST_LOG."
echo "Next step: ./scripts/review/f-b1/regen.sh (heavy N=128) before opening a PR."
```

### Why this is not over-engineering

- The pre-commit hook runs **everything**; `smoke.sh` runs **F-B1 only**, with verbose tracing turned on.
- It is the only place where verbose `RUST_LOG=info,gbf=debug` is the default — useful when debugging a wedge.
- It is the recommended onboarding entrypoint for any new contributor touching F-B1: they run `smoke.sh`, see the trace stream, and can map test names to layers.
- It mechanically codifies the layer-by-layer layout L0 → L1 → L2 → L3 → L4 → L5, which is otherwise documentation-only.

### Documentation

`scripts/review/f-b1/README.md` is extended with a three-tier table making the boundaries explicit:

```markdown
| Script | When to run | Wall clock |
|--------|-------------|------------|
| `cargo test --workspace --all-features` | every commit (auto via pre-commit) | seconds |
| `scripts/review/f-b1/smoke.sh` | every iteration on F-B1 | tens of seconds |
| `scripts/review/f-b1/regen.sh` | before opening a PR; nightly via T-B1.6.10 | minutes |
| `scripts/review/f-b1/verify-packet.sh` | before merging the F-B1 PR | 2× regen.sh |
```

### Logging discipline

- Set `RUST_LOG=info,gbf=debug` by default so structured tracing events from T-B1.0.9 are visible.
- Print the elapsed wall-clock time so a regression in smoke runtime is visible (this is the only time field that uses host wall-clock; it is not part of any artifact).
- Print the exact `cargo test` invocation for each block so a developer can copy-paste a single line to re-run a specific block.

### Anti-patterns to refuse

- Adding heavy `#[ignore]`d tests to `smoke.sh` — they belong to `regen.sh`. T-B1.6.9 (ignored discipline) protects this boundary.
- Setting `RUST_LOG=trace` by default — too noisy for the dev loop.
- Hiding test output with `2>/dev/null` — a smoke test must be diagnosable on first failure.
- Wrapping tests in `|| true` to "keep going" — fail-fast is the point.

### Acceptance gates

```bash
test -x scripts/review/f-b1/smoke.sh
shellcheck scripts/review/f-b1/smoke.sh
./scripts/review/f-b1/smoke.sh   # passes when F-B1 itself is green
```

A separate meta-test in `gbf-meta-checks` asserts that `smoke.sh` is referenced by the README and that its set of `cargo test` blocks does not include any of the heavy `#[ignore]`d test names listed in T-B1.6.9.

### RFC reference

`history/rfcs/F-B1-compute-bringup.md` §13 (task graph), §14 (review packet, scripts directory).

### Dependencies

- T-B1.6.5 (review packet scripts directory exists; smoke.sh joins regen.sh and verify-packet.sh there).
- T-B1.6.2 (bench runner; smoke.sh runs a small subset of its tests).
- T-B1.0.9 (RUST_LOG-visible tracing event vocabulary).
- T-B1.6.9 (ignored discipline guard; smoke.sh must not run `#[ignore]`d tests).

### What this bead does NOT do

- Does **not** replace `cargo test --workspace --all-features` — that is the project-wide gate.
- Does **not** replace `regen.sh` — that owns the heavy N=128 gate.
- Does **not** generate any committed artifact — runs are ephemeral.

For N=128: 64 output tiles, 8 K-tiles each, 1024 panel copies, 65536 output bytes, 2097152 product-accumulate steps. Cross-checks structural counts so failure signal isolates from runtime byte-exact comparison.
